news_summary: Amex Exploration announced discovery of a copper-rich VMS zone at its Perron property, reporting 2.40% Cu over 7.8 m (including 3.92% Cu over 4.4 m) and accompanying Zn/Au/Ag grades. It also mentions a follow-up hole at depth, awaiting assays. The article is copper-focused and exploration-stage. | Antaike said China’s 22 surveyed smelters produced 747,200 tonnes of refined copper cathodes in July, roughly flat versus June, with output expected to rise in August. Copper concentrate treatment charges rose to $57.50/t. The report covers tin separately and includes no aluminium or nickel impact. | The article describes Malaysia’s ongoing phytomining pilot for nickel using hyperaccumulator plants, reporting annual yields of roughly 150–250 kg nickel per hectare and potential scalability. It notes industry interest and planned Indonesia trials, but adoption is still delayed and likely gradual, limiting immediate price impact for nickel.

news_summary: SMM reports secondary copper-based rod producer operating rates fell to 59.53% in July, down 7.59 points month-on-month, amid power curtailment, late-July floods, and furnace repairs in Henan. Tight secondary copper supply forced plants to reduce or suspend output, with August rates expected to decline further.

news_summary: Nickel prices jumped to their highest in two weeks after softer U.S. inflation revived risk-on sentiment and expectations around Fed policy. Strong stainless-steel demand, falling refined output in China, and reduced nickel stocks in both LME and Shanghai supported the rally, with analysts citing a potential market deficit. | SMM data show Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 255,000 wmt to 5.512 million wmt between Aug 6 and Aug 13, with Ni content at 43,300 mt. Stocks at seven major ports also declined. Despite strong demand, stricter pandemic controls slowed unloading, keeping inventories tight.

news_summary: Copper prices fell for a third day to the lowest in almost two months, driven by weak economic data reinforcing demand concerns and nervousness ahead of central bank messaging at Jackson Hole. The selloff is echoed by declines in LME aluminum and nickel, reflecting broader risk-off pressure on industrial metals. | POSCO plans to spend about $200 million (230 billion won) to build a nickel sulfate refining facility to supply growing electric vehicle battery material demand. It also recently acquired a 30% stake in Australia’s Ravensthorpe Nickel Operation, reinforcing its nickel supply and refining expansion plans. | The EU proposes a CBAM on certain imports to prevent carbon leakage, phasing in from 2023 and fully implemented from 2026. It would require importers to buy and surrender certificates based on embedded emissions, using EU ETS auction prices. Aluminium is explicitly in scope; carbon-costs may raise import costs and weigh on demand.

The added 30MW will supply the Kalgoorlie smelter and cut Scope 2 emissions by 30%, building on earlier supply to the Kwinana refinery. The update is focused on decarbonization, not output changes. | SMM says a north China smelter has recently suspended refined nickel production because of high production costs. Nickel salt production continues on separate lines. | Copper prices rose on Monday as risk-on sentiment returned, supported by signs China’s COVID outbreak is tapering and bargain hunting after recent weakness. Additional support came from a labor contract rejection at Chile’s El Teniente mine, raising supply concerns. Aluminium and nickel rose too. | Edgemont Gold Corp. said drilling equipment is on site and Phase I drilling is about to start at its Dungate copper-gold porphyry project in B.C., targeting IP anomalies and a quartz-pyrite breccia zone reported in older drilling. It is an exploration update without assays or production impact, limiting immediate price effects.

news_summary: Nickel prices surged to record highs in Shanghai and a seven-year peak in London, driven by strong demand and dwindling inventories. It also reports aluminium hitting its highest since 2008 on China and Guinea supply worries, while copper rose on improving smelter output and byproduct returns, keeping sentiment firmly bullish. | Nickel surged to record highs in Shanghai and above London’s seven-year peak as inventories dwindled and demand stayed strong, with refined nickel expected to remain in short supply in September. The same piece also highlights elevated prices in aluminium (supply worries in China and Guinea) and copper, reinforcing a broad bullish tone. | Nickel prices surged to the highest level in more than seven years, topping $20,500/tonne (about $9 a pound). The rally is attributed to a supply shortfall as demand from stainless steel mills and EV battery makers rises while exchange-monitored stocks fall sharply, driving a market deficit.

Separately, the article cites aluminium production halts from high electricity prices and a nickel shutdown due to higher energy costs, but LME nickel and aluminium were up on the day. | Nickel prices rose to a two-week high as power shortages and higher electricity costs in China and Europe raised supply fears. Higher energy costs were also cited for aluminium production cuts. | Kosovo’s NewCo Ferronikeli is forced to temporarily stop ferro-nickel production because European electricity prices have surged to record highs. Both shutdowns reduce near-term supply. | Activists and the Fond du Lac Band won multiple federal and state rulings affecting PolyMet’s proposed NorthMet copper-nickel open-pit mine in Minnesota. EPA “may affect” findings and court decisions suspend permits and require further review, heightening delays and potential supply constraints for copper and nickel.

news_summary: Canada Nickel says metallurgical performance at its Crawford project improved, delivering higher nickel recovery (62%, +19% vs prior) alongside better iron and cobalt recoveries. Management links nickel recovery gains to increased project value and continues feasibility work, though assay results are delayed. The news is supportive for nickel sentiment. | Prony Resources, the nickel producer in New Caledonia previously acquired from Vale, said Tesla agreed to a multi-year nickel supply deal for battery material. Prony aims to raise output to about 44,000 tonnes by 2024. Tesla is Prony’s largest customer; negotiations were via Trafigura. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. reported additional drilling results at its 100% owned Kenbridge nickel-copper project in Ontario, including strong Ni grades over 25.5 metres (with associated copper) and additional zone intercepts. TDEM surveys support conductive zone continuity, guiding further drilling toward down-dip and along-strike extensions. | Tartisan Nickel reported additional drilling results from its Kenbridge Ni-Cu project in Ontario, including 25.5 metres of 1.13% Ni with 0.61% Cu, plus higher-grade nickel-copper intervals. Interpretation is based on TDEM-conductive zones and supports ongoing down-dip/along-strike drilling, aiding sentiment for nickel and copper. | Norilsk says Botswana has not implemented an agreed settlement and the dispute over liquidation and sale of BCL’s assets remains in London arbitration. Observers warn PNRB’s plan to acquire and restart the Selebi nickel-copper-cobalt assets may fall through without a resolution, affecting near-term supply outlooks. | Fathom Nickel reported preliminary interpretation of 2021 soil geochemistry from its Albert Lake property, showing multiple anomalous Ni zones with associated Cu and other elements, and noted drilling is ongoing with assays pending. It also received DTC eligibility approval for OTC trading, mainly impacting investor access rather than metal fundamentals. | Bitterroot Resources reported assay results from two holes at its LM Project, stating massive and semi-massive sulphides are enriched in nickel and copper, with disseminated mineralization in one hole. The company continues drilling toward additional targets, but no near-term production or supply disruption is indicated. | Prony Resources said Tesla agreed to buy about 42,000 tons of nickel in a multi-year deal, positioning Prony as a key supplier to the U.S. EV maker. Prony also plans to produce around 44,000 tons of nickel by 2024, doubling its expected 2021 output.

news_summary: Nickel surged to a seven-year high near $10/lb as supply fears intensified. Vale’s Sudbury issues reduced production guidance and output, while Norilsk reported lower refined nickel. Philippines weather and logistics also hurt supply, and Indonesia renewed export-noise concerns. Demand improved with reopening and stainless steel strength. | The US Forest Service proposed a 20-year ban on mining in Minnesota border waters, freezing new permits and blocking Antofagasta’s bimetallic copper-nickel project. The move cuts expected domestic supply for EV-related copper and nickel, potentially tightening near-term market expectations while the approval process is reviewed. | The US Forest Service proposed a 20-year ban on mining in Minnesota border waters, freezing new mining leases for two years and effectively blocking Antofagasta’s bimetallic copper-nickel project. Environmental reviews could extend uncertainty for copper and nickel supply, weighing on price expectations for both metals. | Reuters reports the head of the Philippine Nickel Industry Association said bad weather and heavy rains could cut this year’s Philippine nickel output. Mining and shipping are usually disrupted during the rainy season, but this year’s downpour is more disruptive, and ore shipments are also hindered by a shortage of vessels. | BHP is working to make its Western Australia Nickel West nickel operations lower-carbon, including a solar power purchase agreement for part of Kwinana refinery electricity and studies on mineral carbonation of tailings. The company also seeks higher nickel supply agreements tied to EV batteries, while copper is cited as another growth priority. | Nickel prices have surged to a seven-year high as supply problems intensify. The article links the rally to worsening supply conditions, suggesting tighter near-term availability and strong expectations for continued upward price pressure in nickel markets over the coming days and weeks.

news_summary: LME copper futures dropped over 3% as perceived tightness in nearby supply eased and traders shifted focus to weakening Chinese demand amid growth concerns and power shortages. The piece also notes nickel and aluminium fell more than 5% and 4%, respectively, in a broader risk-off move. | SMM says pure nickel inventories in China’s domestic bonded areas fell this week. Lower domestic spot premiums and prior inventory gains first weakened the SHFE/LME nickel ratio, but reverse arbitrage and low domestic stocks pushed the ratio higher. Over 1,000 mt arrived, and some bonded stock was moved to domestic markets.

Plant inventories are adequate despite power rationing, while port stocks build. Restocking demand is weaker and smelting costs remain high, leading to expected stable prices. | SMM reports the SHFE/LME nickel price ratio continued falling as domestic nickel plate inventories declined more slowly, alongside large imported inflows. news_summary: LME copper closed down $37/t to $9,458.5 after uncertainty ahead of the Fed meeting and worries about China’s slowdown. Traders expected potential scaling back of QE and focused on the dollar outlook. Copper also saw weak warehouse positions, while nickel stocks fell to multi-year lows. | BHP and Wyloo are negotiating BHP’s acquisition of Canada’s Noront, driven by the need to secure battery-metal supply. BHP has raised its offer and extended the bid period. The Noront Eagle Nest project contains high-grade nickel (and copper), with delays from government negotiations, keeping supply expectations tight. | London copper rose 1% after the U.S. Fed signaled no immediate interest-rate hike, supporting expectations for sustained economic recovery. The piece cites resilient demand and supply uncertainty. It also notes LME aluminium and nickel gaining modestly, reinforcing a broad supportive macro backdrop for industrial metals. | Transparency Market Research expects nickel sulfate demand to grow (14.7% CAGR) as EV adoption and Li-ion batteries expand, with nickel sulfate used in battery cathodes and electroplating. It also cites growing use in surface treatment and coatings, which may lift expectations for nickel feedstock demand over time.

news_summary: Nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose 60,000 wmt to 9.05 million wmt (71,000 mt Ni content) as of Nov 5, with the Guangxi port driving most of the increase. Despite a recent rise, nickel ore shipments may be ending with the monsoon season in the Philippines, so inventories are expected to fall. | Sherritt said it will boost production of battery-grade nickel at its Moa joint venture in Cuba by 15% to 20%, extending the mine’s life beyond 2040 to meet EV battery demand. It highlighted processing and U.S. embargo risks but expects construction/equipment orders and follow-up next quarter.

news_summary: Vale has ramped down its Copper Cliff Smelter into a “hot” standby state for two weeks to optimize concentrate stock levels after an incident halted production at the Totten Mine. The move pauses ore smelting temporarily, with refinery operations continuing and no customer impact expected in the article. | A fire broke out in a ferronickel smelting furnace at a nickel smelter in Konawe, southeast Sulawesi, Indonesia. The incident affects only one smelting furnace production line, with expected capacity of about 650 mt/month in Ni content under normal operations. The article says the impact is not significant. | SPC Nickel announced staking of a large land package covering Nunavut’s Muskox Ni-Cu-PGM intrusion and acquisition of an extensive historical geoscience database. The release emphasizes future evaluation and potential for feeder-dyke mineralization, but it does not change near-term production, inventories, or trade flows for nickel or copper. | BHP’s executive said a decarbonised world will require much higher metal supply, projecting nearly double copper demand and about four times nickel demand over the next 30 years. The comments were presented as modelling results supporting the long-run investment case, potentially boosting short-term sentiment.

news_summary: Surge Battery Metals announces it has closed an option acquisition to earn an 80% interest in nickel claims (HN4 and N100) in Northern British Columbia from Nickel Rock Resources. The company issued shares and plans $200,000 in exploration expenditures over two years, with further joint-venture steps pending. | Merger talks between IGO Ltd and nickel producer Western Areas showed no progress at annual meetings, with the deal now past three months. The update highlights a new copper-zinc mineralization style but is unlikely to materially shift near-term metals prices beyond copper expectations. | Nornickel said it settled a dispute with Botswana’s government and BCL Group tied to its aborted Africa asset sale, including a 50% stake in the Nkomati joint venture mine. The company received agreed cash compensation and both sides waived claims. The resolution may lessen near-term nickel supply uncertainty.

news_summary: Nickel neared seven-year highs as LME on-warrant inventories fell to 62,304 tonnes from over 200,000 in April, signaling tighter supply. The rally was reinforced by expectations that China may provide fiscal support despite property-sector distress, boosting demand outlook; prices later eased slightly from peaks. | Nickel and other industrial metals rose as optimism about China’s property sector returned. The headline implies improved demand expectations for construction-related materials, providing near-term support to prices. By extension, the move is likely to affect copper and aluminium alongside nickel. | Chinese nickel ore inventories at major ports fell 114,000 wmt week-on-week to 9.35 million wmt as of Nov 26, with Ni content at 74,300 mt. The decline is linked to fewer shipments during the Philippines wet season, alongside recovering NPI production. Further near-term inventory declines are expected from slower supply and stronger demand. | TrendForce reports Indonesia intends to end exports of unprocessed nickel ores (and also mentions copper and tin), which could disrupt global raw-material supply chains for EV batteries. With rising nickel demand for NCM cathodes, potential tightening supports expectations for higher nickel prices within weeks, though copper effects are less direct.

As the Philippines is a key nickel ore supplier to China, the suspension could further tighten global nickel supply amid an ongoing INSG-noted supply gap. | Copper rose about 1% on the LME as Omicron demand concerns eased and investors regained risk appetite. The piece cites low warehouse inventories as extra support. It also flags Chile royalty bill progress, nickel mine depletion, and aluminium’s tight nearby supply via 14-year-low stocks and a cash premium.

news_summary: Huayou Cobalt commissioned its hydrometallurgical nickel smelting project in Indonesia at PT. Huayue Nickel Cobalt after completing equipment installation and testing. Using laterite ore, it targets 60,000 mt of nickel and 7,800 mt of cobalt annually. The first batch has been produced and performance indicators are qualified. | Copper prices slipped as Omicron fears and slowing China growth reduced demand outlooks. LME copper was down 0.3% on the day and around 4% on the week, despite strong year-to-date performance. China growth and property guidance stayed cautious, while aluminium inventories rose 16% and other base metals moved mixed. | Huayou Cobalt announced that its Indonesia Morowali laterite nickel wet-smelting project (60,000 t/y nickel) has completed installation, entered trial production, and produced a first batch of products with qualified indicators. The update is expected to improve the company’s nickel self-supply and integration, lifting nickel sentiment. | Huayou Cobalt said its Indonesian smelting joint venture reached first trial production, with product quality meeting requirements and producing mixed hydroxide precipitate that can be processed into nickel and cobalt battery chemicals. It plans large annual nickel output using HPAL, but no full-production timeline was given. | Canada Nickel reported metallurgical testwork at its Crawford nickel sulphide project, achieving 61% nickel recovery and 37% nickel concentrate grade from lower-grade samples, above PEA modeled performance. The company says the optimized flowsheet improves recovery and will proceed with variability testing for feasibility. | China’s Chengtun plans to invest US$245 million in an Indonesian nickel matte project. The move points to future processing capacity for nickel feedstock in Indonesia, which can increase expectations for supply and likely pressure nickel prices in the near term as traders price in potential output growth. | Gungnir Resources announced final 2021 drill results from its Lappvattnet nickel deposit in Sweden, reporting high-grade nickel intersections including 6.52% Ni over 0.8 metres within a wider mineralized zone. The company also notes surface EM geophysics underway and expects new assays to feed future resource updates. | Russia’s Nornickel increased its investment guidance by billions of dollars, citing construction cost inflation and expanded modernization for energy and production assets in the Arctic. It plans upgrades including new/expanded concentrators and smelter capacity, plus a new copper production facility in Monchegorsk, alongside Arctic logistics debottlenecking.

news_summary: Hongsheng International Resources, a Chengtun Mining Group subsidiary, plans to form Chengmai Nickel in Indonesia via a JV. The project targets a high-grade nickel matte output of 40,000 mt/year in nickel content, with total investment of $350m. Funding will be staged based on requirements. | Shengtun Mining plans to build a high-nickel matte project in Indonesia’s Weidabe Industrial Park with 40,000 tons/year capacity, investing about $350 million. It is intended to supply battery-grade nickel sulfate for ternary batteries, with product underwritten by JV partners. This expands nickel-related output and feedstock expectations. | The piece notes that nickel is edging above $20,000/t, implying improving near-term sentiment for nickel. It also references aluminium supply staying intact despite output curtailments and mentions severe shipping disruption through the Strait of Hormuz, factors that can affect aluminium transport and pricing. | SMM reported Chinese port nickel ore inventories fell 169,000 wmt to 9.18 million wmt (72,100 mt Ni content) by Dec 3. Seven major ports combined declined 129,000 wmt. Backlogs and slow shipments at Lanshan and shipping difficulties at Jingtang reduced arrivals, and inventories are expected to continue sliding short-term. | China’s Chengtun plans a $245m investment in an Indonesian nickel matte project (PT ChengMach Nickel), producing about 40,000 tpa on a nickel-content basis. The JV, with Chengtun majority ownership, adds to Indonesia’s nickel feedstock pipeline, potentially improving supply expectations for nickel processing. | SMM reports the SHFE/LME nickel price ratio rose this week after prior weakness. Renewed COVID-19 fears have raised concerns about overseas nickel demand, while China’s demand stays stable and nickel arrivals fall. Inventories declined, but planned shipments of nickel briquette and plate could lift supply, limiting upside.

news_summary: LME nickel stocks have fallen sharply since April and premiums remain wide, indicating tight prompt availability. China’s refined nickel imports accelerated since April, reaching record monthly highs and strengthening demand amid constraints from maintenance, product shifts, and energy-related disruption. The market supply gap is widening, keeping nickel tight. | SMM reports Nornickel’s nickel balance update: 2021 tightened sharply due to supply cuts and strong demand recovery, but expectations shift to a nickel surplus of about 59,000 mt in 2022 (mainly low-grade). High-grade may be balanced with possible shortages, while EV battery demand supports longer-term growth. | Since Q4, primary nickel supply has been tight and prices strong, but the article anticipates an off-season slowdown. Refined nickel supply eases gradually, nickel sulfate demand faces weaker momentum, and stainless steel production trends remain under pressure, limiting upside drivers into December/1Q. | Azimut and Mont Royal report discovering a highly prospective nickel-copper ultramafic intrusion at Wapatik. The headline suggests potential future mineralization, but the report is exploratory and does not indicate immediate changes to mine output, inventories, or refining capacity. Near-term price impact on nickel and copper is therefore limited.

news_summary: Canada Nickel released improved metallurgical testing for its 100%-owned Crawford Project in Ontario, reporting 61% nickel recovery and 37% nickel concentrate from lower-grade samples. The company is starting further variability testing for its feasibility study, citing recovery gains versus the earlier PEA and potential for enhanced flowsheet performance and future supply. | EV Nickel reported high-grade nickel intersections at its Langmuir Project, including 8.66% Ni over 1.11m and other significant intervals, plus calculated nickel tenors of 18% to >40% in sulphide. Metallurgical testing is pending, but the results bolster expectations for future nickel production. | Copper fell for a third day as Omicron concerns offset China’s stimulus effects. A stronger yuan reduced costs for buyers, but investors cut Comex longs and remained cautious. Tight Chinese stockpiles and strong imports were supportive, while rising LME availability and weak deliveries tempered gains. | LME nickel inventories are falling and cash premiums have risen, while Shanghai refined nickel stocks remain extremely low. China has accelerated imports of refined nickel and nickel intermediates since April, driven by tight domestic supply and booming demand (stainless and battery-grade). Analysts expect nickel to stay tight until new supply arrives, supporting higher prices. | Australia’s Nickel Mines agreed to buy a 70% stake in Indonesia’s Oracle Nickel Project for $525m, including $371m equity purchase and $154m shareholder-loan funding. The RKEF-based project targets 36,000 tpa nickel-in-NPI capacity plus a 380MW captive power plant, with operations starting in 2023.

news_summary: China’s customs data show nickel ore and concentrate imports in November at 4.0076 Mt, down 10.47% from October but up 12.08% versus a year earlier. Shipments from the Philippines were the majority, also lower m/m, with rainy-season conditions reducing transport volume, affecting near-term supply expectations for nickel. | SMM cites Chinese customs data showing refined nickel imports of 26,069 mt in November. While imports fell 24.4% month-on-month, they more than doubled versus a year earlier (+102.5% YoY). January–November imports were also up sharply year-on-year, signaling higher nickel inflows. | Spot nickel import profits are positive (~250 yuan/mt) as traders move cargoes from bonded inventories into China. However, Shanghai inventory growth is limited because some cargoes were presold. Domestic pure nickel inventories are expected to stay low next week, with only 1,500–2,000 mt arrivals, keeping the SHFE/LME ratio high. | Chinese ports’ nickel ore inventories fell 118,000 wmt to 8.66 million wmt as of Dec 24, with Ni content at 68,000 mt. Inventories have declined for five straight weeks after reduced imports, though downstream demand remains weak due to high costs and restrictions. Further declines are expected before Chinese New Year.

news_summary: Tesla has signed a nickel concentrate supply deal with Talon Metals from the Tamarack Nickel Project in Minnesota. Tesla plans to buy about 75,000 tons of nickel concentrate over six years at LME-listed prices, supporting battery-material sourcing and potentially tightening near-term nickel supply expectations. | BHP will invest about $100m in Tanzania’s Kabanga nickel project, including buying an 8.9% stake with potential to raise it further. The move reflects a shift back to nickel amid green-energy demand. Kabanga also targets copper production, but output is planned from 2025 onward, limiting near-term price impact. | Tesla signed its first US nickel supply deal to buy 75,000 tonnes of nickel over six years from Talon Metals’ Tamarack mine in Minnesota, with options to increase tonnage. The contract supports nickel demand visibility and development funding, likely boosting sentiment for nickel prices in the near term. | SMM reports nickel prices pulled back but remain supported by low refined nickel inventories and continued stock declines. Spot premiums over futures expanded and the import window stayed open, easing tight supply. Nickel sulfate demand and restocking activity were strong, with fewer producers cutting output, leaving few bearish factors. | Resources & Energy reports petrographic confirmation of magmatic Ni-Fe sulphides at its Springfield (East Menzies) project, identifying nickel minerals such as pentlandite and related sulphides in RC drill samples. The company expects further multi-element and PGE/gold assays in coming weeks, but no production or supply change is announced. | Dundas Minerals says its maiden drilling at the Kokoda gold prospect will finish this week, and the rig will move to the nearby Jumbuck nickel prospect. Jumbuck has historic anomalous nickel grades (0.5% in a 2011 hole) with untested conductive anomalies, and drilling is expected to start this weekend. | Tesla signed its first U.S. nickel supply agreement with Talon Metals, tied to nickel concentrate prices on the London Metals Exchange. The deal sources from Talon’s Tamarack project in Minnesota and is aimed at more environmentally friendly battery materials. Tesla plans to buy 75,000 tonnes over six years. | Nickel prices held near two-month highs as electric-vehicle battery demand boosted offtake and reduced nickel stocks in LME-approved warehouses. LME inventory has fallen sharply since April 2021, with high cancelled warrants and a wider cash premium versus the three-month contract indicating tightening supply. Aluminium is also mentioned. | Tesla said it signed its first U.S. nickel supply deal with Talon Metals, selecting the Tamarack mine in Minnesota for its EV battery material. Tesla plans to buy 75,000 tonnes of nickel over six years and will also purchase cobalt and iron ore from Talon. | LME nickel futures gained around 3% to $21,500/tonne, the highest since May 2014. Prices rose as electric-car battery demand reduced LME nickel inventories, which fell about 62% since April 2021. Warehouse stock levels were 99,954 tonnes and nearly half were write-off receipts, signaling continued supply pressure. | SMM reports Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed around Jan 11. Nickel led with a 3.45% gain, supported by falling SHFE inventories, LME nickel and tight spot pure-nickel supply. Aluminium rose 1.97% with inventories down, despite weakening demand. Copper was essentially flat, though LME copper inventory fell. | SMM’s daily review reports mixed metal moves in Asia: Shanghai nickel rose more than 3% and stainless steel also jumped, while Shanghai aluminium climbed nearly 2%. Copper edged down slightly (~-0.01%). The piece attributes some nickel support to low inventories and improved spot tightness. | France plans to raise €1bn to support industries in securing battery raw materials amid soaring prices, including nickel. The government intends to reduce dependence on nickel, cobalt and lithium outside the EU and push Renault and Stellantis toward domestic battery production. It also seeks investment in processing projects such as in Dunkirk. | Talon Metals agreed to supply Tesla with 75,000MT of nickel concentrate from its Tamarack project, with pricing linked to the LME nickel cash settlement. The deal is conditional on reaching required stake and starting commercial production by 2026. Tesla also holds rights to negotiate additional supply, supporting nickel demand visibility. | Tesla signed a deal with Talon Metals to source carbon-neutral nickel from a Minnesota plant. Talon expects to sell at least 165 million pounds of nickel to Tesla over six years, with pricing linked to the LME nickel official price. The announcement signals firmer nickel demand expectations. | Tesla has signed a deal to source nickel from a Minnesota mine. The agreement links Tesla’s battery supply chain to a specific nickel source, potentially tightening expectations around available supply for the near term and supporting nickel pricing sentiment over the coming days to weeks. | United Steelworkers praised Talon Metals’ six-year nickel supply agreement for the Tamarack Project in central Minnesota. The union highlighted the deal’s role in providing customer stability and supporting domestic sourcing for battery-related nickel demand, reinforcing expectations for nickel supply continuity. | Talon Metals and Tesla agreed on a supply deal for nickel from the Tamarack project in Minnesota. Tesla will buy 75,000 tonnes of nickel in concentrate over six years, with pricing linked to the LME nickel cash settlement. The contract supports demand sentiment for EV battery nickel. | Tesla agreed to buy 75,000 tonnes of nickel concentrates from Talon Metals’ Tamarack project, with pricing linked to the LME cash-settlement nickel price. The supply contract strengthens near-term nickel demand sentiment as automakers secure battery-material inputs. Tamarack also involves copper, but impact is mainly nickel-focused.

news_summary: SMM expects nickel prices to rise in January, citing tight spot supply and strong fundamentals. Global inventories are low, LME nickel remains in backwardation, and domestic spot premiums stay elevated. Pre-CNY importing is limited, while nickel sulphate trading is active due to stockpiling and smelter output cuts, supporting demand. | Nickel starts the year at a 10-year high, up nearly 6% YTD, driven by falling LME exchange stocks and tight Class 1 availability. Demand support comes from rapid EV and battery growth (boosting nickel sulfate needs), while supply risk persists after smelter disruption and constrained feedstocks. | SMM’s midday review shows downside pressure across most non-ferrous markets. Shanghai aluminium fell about 2.18% at midday, while Shanghai copper dropped around 0.78%. Shanghai nickel ultimately recovered and closed up about 0.97%. The US dollar eased slightly, but demand/spot activity remained soft, weighing on prices. | Indonesia’s deputy minister said the government may start taxing exports of ferronickel and nickel pig iron in 2022. A 2% tax is likely if nickel prices are above $15,000/tonne and increases with higher nickel prices, aiming to drive downstream investment. The measure could curb export supply. | Singapore’s High Court dismissed businessman Ng Yu Zhi’s bid to stay a $23m civil suit tied to an alleged $1 billion nickel trading scam. The case stems from a purported investment scheme run by his firms, while he faces 75 criminal charges. The report does not mention physical nickel supply or trading disruptions. | SMM said nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 78,000 wmt week-on-week to 8.59 million wmt as of Jan 14 (67,500 mt Ni content). Arrivals of laterite ore were low, and downstream purchasing before Chinese New Year was weak, leaving port stock levels tight. | SMM reviews 2021 nickel inventory trends: domestic stocks fell after mid-May, bonded-area nickel inflows slowed near year-end, and LME inventories slid down as nickel beans and plates flowed into China. It describes tight spot pure nickel and expects 2022 de-stocking, though at a slower pace later.

news_summary: Boliden says nickel production at its Harjavalta smelter has resumed after a December 24 stoppage from a slag explosion. Output will be ramped back to the site’s annual feed capacity of about 370 ktonnes. No injuries were reported, signaling reduced near-term supply disruption risk for nickel. | The article reports nickel breaking above $23,000/t on the LME, signaling near-term bullish momentum. SMM cites low inventories and insufficient warehouse receipts as key contradictions, amplified by shocks including Myanmar’s Dagong Mountain power-plant bombing, higher European carbon prices/Indonesia carbon tax, and Indonesia’s nickel export tariffs, raising supply-side concerns. | Nickel is in the most severe squeeze in over a decade as LME inventories fall and the market scrambles for battery-grade “Class 1” nickel. LME three-month prices have surged above $22,000/t, cash premiums widen sharply, backwardation spikes, and Shanghai stocks hit multi-year lows amid rising demand and deficits. | Nickel surged to a 2011 peak on very low exchange inventories, with Russia-related sanctions threats adding supply concerns. Markets also anticipate stronger EV battery demand. LME three-month nickel rose sharply, and traders linked the broader rally to deficits ahead; aluminium was also cited as exposed to supply risk. | Nickel prices surged, helped by LME inventory drawdowns to multi-year lows and expectations of higher EV battery demand. The LME three-month contract hit the highest since 2011, while traders also flagged supply concerns, including a larger projected nickel deficit. Sanctions fears over Russia are also expected to widen aluminium supply gaps. | Indonesia’s Ministry of Energy targets higher ferronickel production in 2022 (to 1.67 million tons), including increased nickel pig iron and nickel matte. It also forecasts a slight increase in electrolytic copper production. The news is about production targets, implying limited near-term price impact. | SMM says Shanghai nonferrous metals closed mixed amid US inflation pressure. SHFE copper rose 0.64% as copper inventories fell on the week; aluminium fell 0.28% due to resumed capacity in Yunnan/Qinghai and weaker pre-CNY demand; nickel surged 4.21% on low inventories and backwardation. | Chinese ports’ nickel ore inventories fell 142,000 wmt to 8.45 million wmt (66,400 mt Ni content) as of Jan 21, with laterite imports dropping in December due to wet-season disruptions. Despite weaker downstream buying, inventory is set to remain on a downward trend, supporting nickel prices.

PCAM makers plan to keep running and maintain raw material demand post-holiday, while nickel intermediates/matte remain limited. Nickel briquette demand stays resilient, inventories fall, and SHFE/LME briquettes decline, supporting backwardation and bullish nickel futures. | The Biden administration cancelled two mineral leases for Antofagasta’s Twin Metals proposed copper-nickel mine in Minnesota, halting the project. The Interior Department said renewals were improper under regulations. Twin Metals plans to challenge the decision. The move complements a longer Minnesota mining ban proposal, affecting expected North American supply. | Grid Metals reports further 2021 drilling results from its Bannockburn nickel sulfide property, including wide nickel-enriched ultramafic intervals (e.g., 193.5m at 0.31% Ni) and higher-grade sections near 0.40–0.41% Ni. The B Zone target is widened and remains open for further drilling; assay results for remaining holes are pending. | SMM commentary links hawkish Fed rhetoric to a stronger dollar but says nickel is starting to rise as the Philippines enters the rainy season. It highlights Indonesia considering export tariffs on nickel goods (policy still uncertain). Reduced refinery schedules and weaker stainless procurement are noted, yet expectations point to short-term higher nickel prices. | Electric Royalties Ltd. announced the closing of its Rana nickel project royalty acquisition, securing a 1% net smelter revenue royalty tied to exploration licenses in Northern Norway, including the former producing Bruvann Nickel mine. The transaction is funded with shares and cash and remains subject to customary closing conditions. | Wolfden filed an NI-43-101 technical report for its Rice Island nickel project and reiterated a mineral resource estimate using a 0.5% NiEq cut-off. The release includes upcoming expansion drilling to test extensions of nickel mineralization and references copper grades within the NiEq calculation. | The Philippines ended its 2017 open-pit mining ban, aiming to revive a mining sector hurt by the pandemic. GlobalData says the ban delayed projects, including the King-King mine. Now-allowed open-pit projects are forecast to add copper and nickel production, potentially improving near-to-medium-term supply expectations.

news_summary: LME base metals rose Wednesday, with aluminum leading after easing Russia–West/Ukraine tensions and renewed focus on energy supply stress that reportedly shut some smelters in China and Europe. Shanghai pricing also skewed higher for aluminum and copper, while nickel and zinc were slightly lower. Fed minutes and a weaker dollar supported metals. | Andrew Forrest backed IGO’s bid to acquire nickel miner Western Areas, pledging his company Wyloo’s 9.8% stake to support the merger. The support also reinforces IGO’s plan to build a nickel processing plant in Australia, potentially improving expectations for nickel processing and supply continuity, supportive for nickel sentiment. | SMM expects ongoing destocking with LME nickel inventories declining since January. Nickel sulphate prices rebound as supply tightens after loss-making producers cut output, and domestic raw-material shortages may worsen as plants resume. Meanwhile, stainless steel mills restarting should lift NPI demand, while limited high-grade matte supply caps nickel feedstock growth. | SMM says China’s social inventory of refined nickel and bonded-area nickel both fell sharply in January to multi-year lows. Downstream buyers stocked ahead of the CNY holiday, while imports into bonded areas declined after the window opened. Nickel prices rose alongside strong inventory drawdowns, supporting bullish sentiment. | Copper prices fell on the London Metal Exchange as heightened Russia–Ukraine tensions lifted risk-off sentiment, strengthened the dollar, and raised costs for non-US buyers. The report cites easing China import premiums and rising copper stockpiles. Russia is a major producer of aluminium and nickel, though quoted moves are mixed. | Nickel is reported to be climbing after the U.S. renewed warnings about a potential Russian invasion. The renewed geopolitical risk is likely prompting market participants to price in disruptions or supply tightness related to Russia, increasing nickel’s risk premium and supporting higher prices in the near term. | SMM reports China Nonferrous Metals Industry No. 6 Metallurgical Construction won bids for two sections of Indonesia OBI Nickel-Cobalt Wet Phase III. The project includes laterite nickel wet smelting and nickel-cobalt refining activities (smelting and nickel/cobalt hydroxide processing), signaling incremental refining capacity buildout.

news_summary: Nickel prices jumped to the highest level in more than a decade as electric-vehicle demand tightened lithium-ion battery cathode supplies. The Ukraine conflict raised additional supply-flow concerns, with Russia a top nickel producer. The article also notes Russia’s aluminum production role and suggests aluminum prices are nearing new highs. | Nickel and aluminium surged to multi-year highs after Russia’s troop move in eastern Ukraine raised fears of sanctions and export disruptions. Russia supplies a meaningful share of global production. With inventories tight and LME premiums/stocks deteriorating, the market expects near-term supply risk, supporting higher prices. | Nickel and aluminium reached multi-year highs after Russia’s move into eastern Ukraine raised concerns about sanctions and interruptions to Russian exports. Russia accounts for about 7% of mined nickel and 6% of global aluminium. With already undersupplied markets and falling LME stocks, prices rose sharply. | SMM reports that the US may impose sanctions on Russia. If so, refined nickel previously destined for the US could instead be redirected to China. Russian Nornickel output is steady near 210,000 mt, and Russia already supplies sizable volumes to China, so redirected flows could increase supply and weigh on nickel prices. | SMM data shows Indonesia exported 6,087 mt of high-grade nickel matte in January (4,748 mt in metal content), mainly to Japan with a smaller flow to China. The volume was below previous years’ typical 7,500–8,500 mt/month range, suggesting tighter near-term nickel matte supply. | SMM reports SHFE and LME nickel diverging and notes wider import losses. It argues that if US sanctions target Russia, Nornickel refined nickel could reroute toward China and/or enter LME warehouses, tightening US/Europe supply while boosting LME stocks, premiums, and opening import windows for nickel. | LME aluminium stayed near a 13-year high as investors worried that western sanctions and the halted Russia gas pipeline could disrupt Russian metal exports. Russia supplies ~6% of aluminium production and 7% of nickel mining. Nickel also hit multi-year highs amid tight inventories and higher spot premiums, with copper modestly higher. | Aluminium and nickel eased after recent multi-year highs, but prices stayed elevated as the US, EU and UK consider tighter sanctions on Russia. With Russia producing key shares of global aluminium and mined nickel, traders fear supply impairment. Tight inventories and widening nickel/cash premiums further support bullish sentiment. | Aluminium and nickel pulled back from recent highs, but Western sanctions efforts against Russia are boosting prices by raising fears of impaired Russian metal supply. Russia accounts for about 6% of aluminium and 7% of mined nickel. Tight markets persist, with higher nickel premiums and falling LME inventories.

news_summary: Nickel prices hit their highest level in more than a decade as EV battery deployment accelerates and demand for high-nickel cathode inputs surges. The LME delivery incentive and low exchange inventories tighten physical supply, keeping premiums and backwardation elevated. Sanctions risk around Russia further supports the bullish tone. | Putin’s decision to take military actions in Donbass heightened commodity-market fears. Gold rose, while nickel and aluminium “reacted more drastically” due to concerns that war could disrupt exports. The piece notes Russia supplies over 15% of global pure nickel, implying nickel prices could surge further if exports are affected. | Aluminium prices surged to a record high as Russia’s invasion of Ukraine raised fears of sanctions cutting supplies from major Russian producer and disrupting energy (gas) needed for smelting. The LME three-month aluminium contract jumped 4.6% and European/U.S. physical premiums rose. Nickel also spiked amid supply-tightening expectations. | SMM reports LME nickel cancelled warrants have eased only slightly, remaining elevated at 42,228 mt or 51.94% of total, while registered warrants continue falling to 39,072 mt. LME nickel inventory saw a minor mid-month recovery, but cancelled-warrant share stayed rangebound, implying supply tightness supportive for nickel. | Aluminium and nickel prices jumped sharply as Russia escalated its actions in Ukraine, with markets expecting sanctions and supply disruptions. The article notes Russia’s large share of aluminium and nickel production, falling LME inventories, and higher cash premiums, suggesting near-term shortages and upward price pressure for both metals. | Aluminum prices rose about 20% and nickel about 22.7% as Russia escalated actions in eastern Ukraine. news_summary: Oil falls and risk appetite improves as markets react to Russia-Ukraine peace talks and easing inflation expectations. A key focus is nickel: LME trading resumes within 24 hours after a one-week halt, with new rules including daily price limits and position disclosures. China lockdowns and softer demand weigh on commodities. | LME nickel trading resumes after a rare shutdown caused by wild nickel swings, including a surge after Tsingshan bought large volumes to reverse lower-price bets. LME imposed 5% daily price limits, cancelled March 8 trades, and extended delivery deadlines amid ongoing concerns over Russia-related nickel supply constraints and margin support. | Spot and futures trading ahead of LME nickel opening shows tight nickel supply, with several refined nickel grades still priced at premiums to SHFE, while activity remains light due to pricing disagreement. The key driver is persistent LME–SHFE spread and market concern that any LME surge could lift SHFE nickel and support stainless demand. | Sanctions on Russia coincided with a nickel trading crisis as Tsingshan Holding’s short positions incurred massive losses when nickel surged on the LME, leading to margin-call stress and the exchange’s closure. Banks agreed a standstill on margin calls, and the LME plans to reopen with tighter disclosure and daily price limits. | CGTN reports nickel prices surged on Russia-Ukraine-related supply risks and a Tsingshan-led short squeeze that suspended London trading. Analysts say China’s EV sector is largely insulated because much nickel goes to stainless steel and China can import from Australia, Japan and others. EV nickel demand remains a key medium-term risk. | Most base metals rose as traders weighed supply risks and hopes for additional stimulus in China. LME copper and aluminium were up early in the session. Nickel’s LME trading is set to resume after its March 8 suspension following a rapid price spike, potentially capping near-term moves. | Nickel prices fell by the LME’s new 5% daily limit, reaching a limit-down move as electronic trading reopened. Trading was then halted shortly after opening while the exchange investigated a potential issue with the limit-down band, signaling strong immediate bearish pressure on nickel. | LME nickel trading resumed on March 16 after a more-than-week suspension. Trading was halted March 8 after a Chinese metal tycoon incurred billions of dollars in losses from a large short position, prompting an unprecedented exchange action. A rescue deal was reached, allowing trading to restart. | The article reports that nickel fell to the LME’s exchange limit when trading resumed after the weekend. Such a move implies investors are reacting strongly to bearish catalysts over the non-trading period, creating immediate downward pressure on nickel prices. | The LME suspended trading of some nickel contracts due to an LMEselect “system error,” with trades at or above the lower daily limit kept and some below-limit trades cancelled. The exchange is investigating and aims to reopen as soon as possible, following prior suspensions during a sharp nickel price spike. | BNN Bloomberg’s headline says there is “fresh chaos for nickel” as the LME is hit by a glitch while nickel prices plunge. This points to immediate disorder and weakness in nickel trading conditions, likely driving short-term bearish sentiment for nickel prices. | London nickel trading was suspended again after a short period of resumed activity, according to the article. The interruption can disrupt price discovery and liquidity in the nickel market, potentially creating short-term volatility, but the report provides no clear bullish or bearish fundamental driver. | The article headline reports “fresh chaos” for nickel, saying an LME glitch hit and prices plunged. Such an event suggests immediate downside pressure and heightened volatility in nickel benchmark pricing, at least in the very near term, driven by market disruption rather than fundamentals. | Nickel trading briefly resumed on the LME after a Ukraine-crisis pause but was quickly halted again after the contract hit the new five-percent daily limit-down band. The exchange suspended electronic trading to investigate the limit-down band, amid continuing volatility from supply concerns. | Great Atlantic Resources reports completion of a diamond drilling program at its Mascarene Property’s Wheal Louisiana Cu-Ni-Co occurrence in New Brunswick. Four of five holes intersect chalcopyrite-bearing quartz/carbonate veins. Results will be sampled for gold and multi-element analysis including copper and nickel; no production or supply changes are stated. | The article discusses nickel’s surge during the Ukraine crisis, noting that sanctions on Russia disrupted supply chains and helped drive extreme volatility. It cites LME nickel contract prices rising sharply and triggering a trading halt, illustrating immediate bullish pressure on nickel prices. news_summary: LME nickel trading resumed but was almost immediately halted again after nickel fell through the 5% daily limit-down band. The market had previously been suspended after a rapid doubling of nickel in minutes. Only small volumes traded, and the LME paused electronic trading to investigate the limit mechanism. | SMM reports LME nickel prices fell sharply to the lower limit, with SHFE nickel expected to stay rangebound. Pandemic-related logistics constraints made pure nickel supply in Shanghai rare, while high nickel prices caused downstream nickel sulphate and battery producers to cut output. Weak demand and high costs kept trading subdued. | Shanghai copper and aluminium hit one-week highs as China signaled more market-friendly policies and investors adopted a risk-on stance. LME copper and LME aluminium were also higher, reflecting improved demand expectations amid relaxed production limits. Nickel’s reported moves were mixed, with ShFE down despite broader support for metals. | The article discusses the March LME nickel futures incident, describing a 248% two-day surge in LME nickel that disrupted LME trading and drove large, volatile moves in Shanghai nickel. It attributes the disruption to risk-control failures and contract-market structure far from production/consumption areas, and proposes improving China’s nickel supply-chain risk management. | The London Metal Exchange attempted to restart nickel trading after a week of limbo, but a technical glitch forced a shutdown within a minute of opening. When trading resumed, there were very few deals, leaving the nickel market in disarray and potentially disrupting short-term price formation. | China Non-Ferrous Metals Industry Association officials discuss wildly fluctuating nickel prices and downstream order disruptions. They say nickel is vital for stainless steel and new energy batteries, and that China’s government has previously used targeted reserve releases during price spikes (including other metals) to stabilize the chain. They also highlight China’s reliance on nickel imports and investments in Indonesia. | LME nickel trading resumed with adjusted pricing after technical glitches and a prior one-week suspension. Nickel continued to fall sharply, hitting daily limit-down levels with no official settlement price. The market expects further limit-down sessions until equilibrium, with prices on ShFE used as a reference after adjustments. | Nickel prices fell by 8% to the LME daily limit after the London Metal Exchange reopened nickel trading but encountered problems for a second straight day. The issues followed a suspension lasting more than a week, driving sharp near-term selling and bearish expectations for nickel. | LME’s three-month nickel contract opened limit down, falling 8% to around $41,495, with the exchange widening the trading limit versus the prior session. Traders sold on expectations of lower nickel prices for stainless steel and EV batteries, amid recent trading disruptions and shifting supply concerns, including sanctions-related worries. | LME electronic glitches delayed nickel order entry as trading resumed after a suspension following a spike above $100,000/t. Nickel then dropped through multiple limit-down sessions with widened price bands and no official settlement price, while ShFE prices also fell, implying further near-term downside until an equilibrium is reached. | Fastmarkets’ nickel overview highlights volatility from uncertainty in the near-term nickel supply-demand balance, with nickel used mainly in batteries and stainless steel. It notes regional price divergence and the launch of additional price assessments. It also says inflation pressures are hurting mining profits via wages and input costs, without citing a specific immediate supply or demand catalyst. | The LME explains how official prices and closing prices are published during disruption events and how these values are used for settling physically delivered LME nickel contracts and margining LME contracts. It states that nickel prices have risen to an 11-year high due to fears Russia’s nickel exports could be disrupted. The discussion of aluminium is more about sourcing than reported near-term price moves. | LME nickel trading resumed then was suspended again after a 46% slump beyond the daily downside limit, while SHFE prices remain volatile. Such a move signals acute near-term bearish pressure on nickel futures, typically reflecting rapid changes in market sentiment and/or near-term supply-demand expectations. | Nickel’s sharp decline is affecting Western Areas’ takeover prospects, with IGO Ltd facing pressure to adjust its $3.36/share offer after global nickel price moves. The headline suggests falling nickel has weakened deal hopes and market expectations in the near term. | Nickel prices on the London Metal Exchange fell 15% to about $31,380/ton, repeatedly hitting limit-down for a third straight session. The LME broadened trading bands after a March 9 short squeeze and subsequent halts, but nickel still reached lower limits. Russia’s war-linked sanctions raise supply fears and volatility. | Aluminium surged over 5% as Australia banned exports of alumina and aluminium ores to Russia, worsening raw-material supply worries amid disrupted Ukraine flows. Nickel prices fell, hitting the LME lower trading limit for a fourth straight session as selling reflected expectations of lower prices. | Australia banned exports of alumina and aluminium ores to Russia, following sanctions. The ban, plus disrupted shipments from Ukraine, raised expectations of a raw-material shortfall for Russian smelters, pushing aluminium prices more than 5% higher. Separately, nickel fell to lower trading limits amid selling pressure and adjusted LME limits.

This suggests short-term correction after stressed positioning, with the immediate price reaction favoring nickel bulls. No clear details on supply, demand, or broader metal fundamentals are provided in the text shown. | Talon Metals reported new high-grade nickel-copper (plus cobalt) drill intercepts at the Tamarack Nickel Project, including thick shallow sulphide intersections outside the current resource area. The company frames results as deposit expansion progress toward feasibility and a Tesla nickel-concentrate offtake after commercial production. | Class 1 Nickel announced appointments and mobilization of a 35 km ground EM geophysics survey at its Somanike nickel project in Quebec, including the historical Marbridge Mine. The program aims to confirm extensions to known nickel sulphide trends and generate drill targets ahead of an extensive drilling campaign. | Talon Metals reported additional shallow, high-grade nickel and copper intercepts from drilling in the Tamarack Nickel-Copper-Cobalt project’s CGO West area. Results include thick nickel-copper sulphide intervals and expanded mineralization outside the current resource area, supporting future feasibility and an existing Tesla nickel concentrate offtake. | Fastmarkets says its LME Nickel March Average was skewed because the LME suspended trading and published disrupted Officials for specific dates in March (8-21 and 23 March). It points subscribers to LME Notice 22/064 on Averages methodology during disrupted Officials and will review and update further. | Moody’s Analytics economists say Russia’s invasion of Ukraine is disrupting global commodity supply chains, including nickel produced largely in Russia and Ukraine. With output and logistics constrained, market participants face tighter availability and greater uncertainty for nickel used in batteries, supporting expectations for higher near-term prices.

Shanghai copper rose 0.81%, Shanghai nickel declined but narrowed (down 5.57%), and Shanghai aluminium fell slightly (about -0.13%). LME showed only lun nickel down (over 9%) while LME copper rose modestly and aluminium was flat. No new metal-specific driver is detailed. | Britishvolt and VKTR form a joint venture to develop sustainable nickel refining capacity in Indonesia and supply nickel sulphate for EV batteries, with potential battery plant plans. The move targets a secure nickel supply amid surging raw materials prices and Indonesia’s expanding role in laterite processing. | Britishvolt and VKTR announced a joint venture to develop sustainable nickel refining capacity in Indonesia and supply nickel sulphate for high-performance EV batteries, potentially using renewable energy. With Indonesia a major nickel producer, the initiative and broader EV battery projects support expectations for nickel demand. | London copper edged higher for a second session, supported by bargain-buying, but China’s planned Shanghai lockdown and rising COVID cases weighed on demand expectations. In contrast, Shanghai nickel dropped more than 5%. Chile’s Codelco plans a “non-core” asset offering, a minor producer-development signal. | London copper edged higher for a second session, supported by bargain-buying, though COVID-19 spread in China’s key consumer regions and a two-stage Shanghai lockdown raised demand concerns. In contrast, Shanghai nickel dropped over 5%, reflecting stronger negative sentiment and likely weaker near-term outlook for industrial metals demand. | SMM says SHFE nickel has been correcting downward as longs cut exposure and futures pulled back. Pandemic-related delays and weak spot trading limit near-term demand, while nickel briquette and pure nickel transactions stay poor. NPI remains firm only due to high ore/smelting costs, but overall pure nickel demand is weak. | Nimy Resources reports nickel-bearing gossan uncovered during drill-site preparations at its Mons nickel project in Western Australia. Portable XRF readings show up to 0.96% nickel, with additional anomalous copper and cobalt and proximity to older nickel intersections. Further geochemical sampling will test the near-surface extent. | London copper eased as China’s widening COVID-19 outbreak and Shanghai’s planned lockdown heightened concerns over near-term industrial demand. The softer tone on growth weighed on metals sentiment. Shanghai nickel also fell nearly 5%, reflecting the broader downturn in China-linked consumption expectations. | SMM reports a mixed midday session: Shanghai nickel fell sharply to expand its decline to 6.48%, while copper rose modestly (~0.64%) and aluminium ended slightly lower (~-0.15%) after an intraday rally. The piece links moves to a weaker oil complex, a slightly softer dollar, and concerns over China fuel demand. | London copper fell as COVID-19 infections and a planned two-stage Shanghai lockdown in top metals consumer China fueled concerns over near-term industrial demand. Shanghai nickel dropped about 4.7% on the same growth/demand outlook fears. Commentary pointed to potential impacts on Chinese industrial production. | NickelX received shareholder approval to acquire the Cosmos South nickel project in Western Australia’s Wiluna belt. The company will exercise the option, proceed with formal agreements, and earn a 100% interest by funding at least $500,000 of exploration over 24 months. Preliminary work includes airborne magnetic surveys. | Vietnam is expected to see rising demand for battery metals, with nickel benefiting from growing electric-vehicle uptake. The piece notes nickel’s current use in battery applications is set to increase and highlights Vietnam’s nickel resources and plans for additional mining and processing, including factories targeting nickel outputs. | Sumitomo Metal is reported to see global nickel demand for battery applications at 410,000 in 2022. The headline suggests improving demand expectations for battery-related nickel consumption, which can be supportive for nickel prices in the near term, though the excerpt lacks details on timing or supply response. | Inomin Mines reports significant drill results at its Beaver nickel-cobalt property: a 252m interval grading 20.6% magnesium with 0.16% nickel, plus near-surface silver and copper from another hole (0.29% copper over 3m). Discoveries remain open, supporting improved short-term sentiment for nickel and, to a lesser extent, copper supply potential. | Chinese metals recycler GEM sold a 9% stake in its Indonesian battery-grade nickel processing JV to South Korea’s EcoPro BM and agreed funding to support construction. EcoPro will supply loans to the project, which targets 150,000 tons/year nickel sulfate and supplies high-nickel ternary precursors, supporting expected nickel demand. | SMM says SHFE nickel (2204) fell on March 29, with weak trading, lower volume and open interest, and reduced long positions. It attributes pressure to pandemic-related transport and subdued spot demand, weak downstream demand (briquette/dissolution, April sulphate output), and less active stainless steel operations, while watching LME nickel and macro/geopolitics. | The piece argues Russia’s war on Ukraine is adding turmoil to nickel markets and notes Chinese nickel producer Tsingshan Holding Group faces multi-billion-dollar losses. It raises uncertainty around Tsingshan’s nickel mining interests in South Africa, which could affect short-term nickel supply expectations and price direction.

news_summary: The piece examines how nickel turmoil, which included a surge after the Russia-Ukraine conflict, exposed perceived weaknesses in the London Metal Exchange and highlighted risk-control needs for Tsingshan and its banks. It also spotlights Tsingshan’s controlling owner Xiang Guangda amid scrutiny of the episode’s drivers. | SMM market tracking reports broad weakness in China’s non-ferrous complex early March 30: Shanghai nickel fell 2.75% and Shanghai aluminum dropped 1.5% (inner market), while Shanghai copper declined slightly. Overseas trade improves marginally for some contracts, but epidemic/logistics frictions and shifting macro sentiment keep pressure on metals near term. | The LME said it will run an independent review into the nickel trading chaos earlier this month after prices surged more than 50% to about $100,000/tonne and the exchange suspended nickel trading. Trading resumed with new daily price limits and OTC position reporting requirements. | SHFE nickel fell overnight as market participants withdrew capital and adjusted for weaker near-term demand. Downstream buyers stayed cautious amid sharp nickel price swings, import losses tightened briquette supply, and Wuxi stainless procurement was disrupted by pandemic closures. Nickel sulphate output signals also weakened. Nickel volatility and muted transactions persisted. | Sumitomo Metal Mining said global nickel demand for rechargeable battery use will rise over 20% in 2022 to above 410,000 mt, driven by firm EV growth. It also expects the nickel supply shortfall to narrow, as Indonesia’s nickel pig iron output increases. Overall the demand outlook is supportive for nickel. | The article challenges Indonesia’s claim that its raw nickel export ban boosts downstreaming. It argues the policy has reduced mining revenues via forced domestic selling at lower prices, distorts smelter economics through tax holidays, and risks trade retaliation after an EU WTO case. It concludes the evidence is weak. | LME aluminium rebounded after a near 5% prior-session drop as optimism around Russia-Ukraine talks faded, reducing a supply-related risk premium. A softer U.S. dollar supported metals, but China demand worries from a COVID surge capped gains. Russia sanctions remain a key supply overhang for aluminium, nickel and copper. | SMM reports COVID-impacted logistics and ongoing closures affecting ferronickel plants in northern China. Several plants have reduced output or suspended lines, expected to materially cut nickel-iron production in March. Despite high consolidated ferro-nickel supported by costs and tight supply, downstream stainless demand is poor, limiting acceptance of high prices. | Power Nickel reported final drill-hole assays from its Nisk Main nickel sulphate project showing continued high-grade nickel mineralization (with some copper in the intercepts). The company expects to deliver a NI 43-101 compliant resource in Q2 and has budgeted a fully funded 5,000-metre drill program starting later in Q2. | After LME resumed trading in nickel futures, OTC nickel derivative holders still cannot settle expired contracts using the new reference prices. The International Swaps and Derivatives Association is working on a bilateral ISDA template so counterparties can use updated prices for settlement, reducing counterparty settlement problems. | Tesla reportedly signed a secret nickel supply deal with Vale. Such an arrangement can affect nickel pricing by shaping expectations for near-term demand and supply security for battery-related metals. The article provides no deal volumes or terms, so the impact is likely moderate and uncertain. | The LME CEO said the month’s unprecedented nickel price surge threatened market stability and prompted the bourse to take action it had never done before. The report suggests the exchange intervened due to extreme volatility in nickel markets, but does not specify whether the action is intended to curb prices. | Aluminium prices jumped as markets renewed fears of Russian supply disruptions and the dollar weakened. The story cites Russia’s role in aluminium, copper, and nickel supply, potential sanctions and transport issues, and high European gas prices that have forced smelters to cut output. LME aluminium, copper, and nickel rose.

news_summary: After a historic nickel short squeeze drove LME nickel to extreme levels, the exchange suspended trading and later scrapped troubled trades. Trading has reopened with limits and glitches, but volumes and spreads improved. Prices have moved back toward SHFE benchmarks, with LME nickel about 6% lower and stabilizing. | LME aluminium closed lower on the day but posted the biggest quarterly gain since 1988, driven by supply disruptions and higher smelting costs tied to Russia-Ukraine conflict and sanctions. Nickel also surged strongly after recent volatility. The piece adds supportive copper signals, including an export license and weaker Chile output. | SMM reports a contract for mining and transporting nickel laterite in Indonesia and discusses near-term nickel fundamentals. Nickel closed slightly lower and price amplitude narrowed as stainless-steel demand and downstream battery demand weaken amid logistics/logistics disruptions from the epidemic. Tight resources support nickel, implying volatile but not strongly directional moves. | The article argues that EV and high-nickel battery growth will push nickel demand into an “explosive” phase. It cites low global nickel inventories, rising market volatility, and carmakers (Tesla, BYD) signing or reporting nickel supply contracts. It also highlights constraints in producing nickel sulfate, reinforcing bullish expectations for nickel. | Copper dropped as China’s factory activity data signaled sharper deterioration, increasing near-term demand concerns. A firmer U.S. dollar further pressured dollar-priced metals. Investors stayed cautious ahead of U.S. jobs data. Other base metals weakened too, with aluminium slightly lower and nickel down on Shanghai trading. | The Nickel Institute president urged the LME to restore nickel trading to rebuild market stability. Since nickel trading was suspended, the institute says the disruption is damaging reputation, confidence, and could erode demand for nickel. It calls for clarification from the LME as trading resumes. | SMM says China’s downstream nickel industries PMI fell to 49.25 in March, signaling contraction. While stainless steel production recovered after February restrictions, demand weakened: high nickel prices suppressed purchasing volume (composite 50.24; sub-sectors below 50). Electroplating and NI-MH battery firms postponed orders and purchases stagnated. | Copper prices fell 1.1% as investors worried China’s weak factory activity and Shanghai COVID lockdowns would curb demand. LME three-month copper retreated to about $10,264/t. The article cites supply/energy and other metal moves, but copper remains the clearest downside read. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 230,000 wmt to 5.887 million wmt on April 1, leaving total Ni content at 1,800 mt. Port stockpiles remain at relatively low historical levels. As Philippine rains end but shipments cannot ramp quickly, imported ore build is slow, and nickel ore destocking is expected to slow. | LME nickel trading volumes dropped 28% in March, with the decline linked to a shutdown. The sharp volume contraction suggests reduced market participation and potentially disrupted supply and/or processing, which is generally bearish for nickel pricing in the near term as expectations weaken. | Tesla reportedly signed a multi-year, undisclosed nickel supply deal with Vale for nickel sourced from Canada’s Sudbury operations. With Tesla highlighting nickel as a key constraint and EV battery demand rising, the agreement is likely to bolster market expectations for nickel supply and demand, supporting nickel prices amid recent LME volatility.

news_summary: London copper rose for a second session as traders worried about supply from Chile after February output fell 7.5%. Gains were tempered by a firmer U.S. dollar and lingering concerns over Chinese demand, with China holiday/lockdown measures affecting sentiment. LME also plans an independent review into last month’s nickel chaos.

news_summary: SMM reports refined nickel supply remains tight as pandemic conditions reduce China’s refined production, limit customs clearance, and disrupt south-market transport. North tightness and high SHFE–LME spread raise spot premiums and keep downstream restocking constrained. SMM expects domestic nickel prices to be hard to decline near term. | SMM reports that major battery makers increased the proportion of LFP batteries, but also saw a rise in high-nickel NMC share in Q1. Lithium price strength raised procurement costs, leading to slower LFP purchasing, while advance orders boosted high-nickel NMC allocations. NEV demand was only slightly higher. | SMM reports China’s nickel sulphate market ran a 4,825 mt (nickel content) supply deficit in March, extending three consecutive months of deficits. High raw-material costs reduced in-house briquette dissolution, forcing producers to cut output. Battery-grade prices are expected to stay supported in April as tight supply and high demand persist. | Talon Metals purchased three additional drill rigs to expand exploration outside the main resource area at its Tamarack nickel-copper project in Minnesota. The new drilling targets multiple zones to find high-grade nickel-copper mineralization for potential battery supply, but current output is not reported as changing.

news_summary: SMM cites customs data showing China’s nickel sulphate imports fell 16.7% month-on-month to 681 mt in nickel content in February, despite strong year-on-year growth. Exports were very small. The article expects March net imports to drop to 440 mt (-35.3% MoM) due to lower natural days and wider market price spreads. | SMM reports that high-grade NPI prices in March rose with extreme LME and SHFE nickel moves, expanding the nickel-vs-NPI spread and causing downstream to pause purchases. After LME rules and falling nickel prices, the spread narrowed and NPI stabilized in early April. Weak demand suggests NPI may stay range-bound. | SMM’s daily review shows industrial metals mixed on LME but generally higher in China, led by Shanghai nickel (+2.39%) and Shanghai aluminium (~+1.5%). The piece attributes nickel strength to tighter pure nickel supply amid Shanghai epidemic disruptions and weaker demand, while LME copper is slightly up (~+0.45%). | The piece reviews March events in LME nickel trading, noting that trading was suspended and then resumed amid disruption. Framed as a “short squeeze,” the episode likely drove unusual price dynamics. It also describes how these disruptions affected publication and display of LME nickel data on Fastmarkets. | Victory Nickel filed a proposal to restructure unsecured debt under Canada’s Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act, aiming to continue operating. The company also received bonus shares from Silver Elephant Mining related to the Minago Project, which become freely tradable later in 2022. The update is company-specific with no direct nickel supply impact details. | SMM’s overnight wrap says oil rose on possible action to ban Russian exports and the dollar strengthened. Despite macro headwinds, the article cites expectations of China stimulus supporting metals demand. It reports Shanghai copper up about 0.3%, nickel up about 3.49%, and aluminium up about 1.73%.

news_summary: Culpeo Minerals completed induced polarisation surveys at its Quelon iron oxide-copper-gold project in Chile, identifying large geophysical anomalies and stating three zones are compelling drill targets. The company is pursuing earn-in stakes and plans a maiden drilling program. The update is exploratory and does not change near-term copper supply. | SMM reports SHFE nickel has continued rising but expects prices to stay rangebound. Scarce local supply, poor transportation, and tight nickel briquette and nickel sulphate keep premiums elevated. However, pessimism over downstream demand and weak liquidity/hedging on LME cap upside gains. | SMM reports a shortage of domestic pure nickel supply and temporarily suspends certain premium/discount quotes due to futures diverging from spot. Spot prices for Jinchuan and NORNICKEL nickel rose sharply, supported by reduced production and import losses. SHFE nickel futures increased for six straight days, aiming to reopen the import window. | The IMF criticized the LME for cancelling trades during last month’s nickel “pickle” after Russia-Ukraine fears drove nickel to record highs. The cancellations limited losses for Tsingshan but removed gains for other counterparties, prompting investigations by UK regulators and calls to strengthen LME governance.

news_summary: SHFE nickel rose for a sixth straight day, with refined nickel spot prices also climbing sharply. The report attributes gains to domestic smelter production reductions, declining pure nickel inventories, and weaker nickel salt plant output amid losses. Higher SHFE/LME spreads are expected to open the import window, though a pullback is possible if supply improves. | The article cites LME data showing nickel inventories declining sharply to about 72,600 mt, near a two-year low, after a mid-April peak. It also notes SHFE nickel stocks fell 11.96% week-on-week to 7,668 mt. Lower exchange inventories are generally supportive for nickel futures. | London copper edged up as hopes for additional China stimulus offset a firm dollar and growth concerns. A suspension at Peru’s Las Bambas mine added a copper supply-tightness risk. Aluminium and nickel in LME and Shanghai were broadly softer, reflecting uneven sentiment tied to China lockdown-related demand worries. | The IMF urged strengthening London Metal Exchange governance after a nickel-market short squeeze last month. The event triggered a six-day suspension, a 250% price surge in under two days, and billions of dollars in cancelled trades linked to Chinese producer Tsingshan. The IMF cites governance lessons for commodity-market volatility. | SHFE nickel reversed after consecutive gains, dropping 3.04% as spot refined nickel prices also fell. Despite tight pure nickel supply, reduced downstream production from higher raw-material costs and sluggish demand are weighing on prices. The piece expects further weakness once the import window opens and supply improves.
