news_summary: BHP said labour shortages forced it to downgrade output at its Australian nickel business and its Chilean copper operations. The company’s iron ore division is still tracking its full-year target, but the specific nickel and copper production cuts point to near-term supply constraints that could weigh on copper and nickel prices. | BHP said labour shortages have forced it to downgrade output from its Australian nickel division and its Chilean copper division, even as its iron ore operations remain on track. The update signals near-term supply constraints for nickel and copper, which can weigh on short-term price expectations over coming weeks. | WBMS reported that the global refined nickel market ran a supply deficit of 17,400 mt from January to February 2022, with apparent demand exceeding production. Refined nickel output totaled 447,600 mt versus 464,900 mt demand. Chinese and Indonesian smelter output increased, but demand rose faster. | BHP shares fell after the miner said Q3 copper output dropped 6% to 369.7 kt and it cut FY22 copper guidance to 1,570–1,620 kt from 1,590–1,760 kt, citing lower Escondida volumes. BHP also lowered nickel guidance to 80–85 kt due to COVID-19 labor constraints. | SMM reports that SHFE and spot nickel prices drifted lower as spot fundamentals and futures diverged. It suspended nickel premium/discount quotations but kept 1# nickel assessments normal. Spot Jinchuan and Norilsk nickel averages fell about 2,750 yuan/mt, while inquiries improved slightly but trades remained thin; prices may be hard to fall further without higher inventories. | Norilsk Nickel notified ADR holders that Russian federal law will require termination of depositary receipt programs. From April 27, 2022, shares underlying ADRs will become non-voting and no dividends will be paid on those shares. ADR holders face restrictions on trading their ADRs for entitlements.

news_summary: SMM reports China produced 12,900 mt of refined nickel in April (+2.62% MoM, -0.65% YoY). Output matched expectations, supported by more stable LME nickel and resumed operations as raw-material import issues eased and March maintenance ended. May refined nickel production is expected to rise to ~13,400 mt. | China’s nickel sulphate output fell 12.46% on the month in April as nickel salt factories faced high costs and weak demand with near-negative profits, prompting production cuts to support prices. In May, some plants resumed and output rose, but demand for precursors and batteries remains poor, keeping production subdued. | Indonesia’s President Jokowi plans to meet Elon Musk during a US visit, as the country seeks to accelerate development of its nickel industry to supply EV batteries. Tesla representatives have visited Morawali’s nickel production hub, and Indonesia is targeting a full domestic nickel-to-batteries supply chain. The news is supportive for nickel sentiment. | Nickel 28 reported Q1 2022 Ramu production of 8,756 tonnes of contained nickel and lower cash costs of $1.48/lb net of cobalt by-product credits. Management said production is at expected levels as Asia reopens, while sales were temporarily hit by export restrictions and the Olympics, expected to normalize in Q2.

news_summary: Nickel fell alongside a broader mining sell-off, but the piece argues it is positioned to recover sooner than other battery metals. It points to EV battery demand plus existing stainless-steel use, Australian class-one premium supplies, and China’s grade dislocation where NPI and nickel sulphate trade at deep discounts to LME. | SMM reports tight pure nickel supply in April, with overseas sources driving availability and import losses persisting due to weak SHFE/LME spread. Downstream alloy and battery demand softened after renewed pandemic impacts. May is expected to keep supply and demand relatively weak, with limited upward momentum in SHFE nickel prices. | Canada Nickel announced it submitted a preliminary draft Initial Project Description for the Crawford nickel project to Canada’s Impact Assessment Agency after signing impact assessment process agreements with local Indigenous communities. The step initiates a review period and supports regulatory certainty for the project’s development. | Russia has allowed Norilsk Nickel’s ADRs to remain traded abroad. The move can improve market access and sentiment toward the company, a key nickel producer. While it does not directly change nickel supply or demand, the policy easing may provide mild near-term support to nickel pricing.

news_summary: CNGR and Rigqueza plan to invest $1.3 billion in Indonesia to build three nickel-processing plants at Weda Bay, producing 40,000 tons of high-grade nickel matte annually each. The matte is used to make nickel sulfate for battery precursors. No construction timetable was provided. | SMM reports that high raw-material costs are stressing nickel sulphate manufacturers using dissolving nickel briquette and imported MHP. When nickel briquette prices trade above nickel sulphate, some sulphate plants cut briquette sales, lowering briquette spot premiums versus NORNICKEL nickel. Demand use in stainless/alloys appears limited, muting price signals.

news_summary: The article says nickel demand surged 16.2% in 2021, creating a 168,000-tonne supply shortfall. For 2022, usage is expected to rise 8.6% but global production is forecast to jump 18.2% on Indonesia’s new capacity, moving toward a modest surplus and likely lower LME prices, though Class I supply remains disputed. | SMM reports that in early Shanghai trading, SHFE nickel rose 5.36%, while SHFE aluminium gained 1.69% and SHFE copper rose 0.66%. The broader move coincided with a firmer US dollar, expanding Fed balance sheet, hawkish Fed comments, and crude oil weakness tied to growth concerns. | SMM said nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 7,000 wmt week-on-week to 5.363 million wmt (total Ni content -50 wmt). The decline stems mainly from reduced Philippines shipments due to poor weather, alongside faster consumption by low-inventory NPI plants. Further Philippines shipment developments are watched.

news_summary: Aston Minerals says metallurgical testing of nickel-cobalt sulphide samples from its Boomerang target produced saleable concentrate using conventional flotation. The head grades were ~0.51% nickel and 0.016% cobalt, with concentrate grading up to 11.29% nickel. Further optimization work is planned as analysis continues. | SMM says nickel is likely to fall this week. Weak demand and limited bonded-zone customs clearance have kept pure nickel tight, but the import window should lead to more customs clearances. NPI and nickel-sulphate input costs are pressured by lower LME nickel, while stainless-steel downstream activity remains subdued. | China’s imports of nickel intermediaries MHP and matte hit record levels in April, helped by faster NPI-to-matte conversion and ramped MHP supply from Indonesia. With supply feeding sulphate production and incentives to convert into exchange-deliverable Class 1 nickel rising, availability could improve and LME nickel prices/spreads face downside pressure.

news_summary: SMM reports weak downstream demand and declining nickel sulphate prices due to softer raw-material costs and limited alloy buying. However, short-term pure nickel inventories remain low and support prices, while Indonesian Delong NPI commissioning is progressing and some supply restarts. Overall, nickel is expected not to drop sharply. | SMM reports CITIC Futures’ view that China’s nickel market has faced weak demand amid high LME nickel prices and pandemic-related drag. news_summary: LME copper held near a three-week high as China demand optimism rose. Better Chinese factory data and easing COVID-19 lockdowns, including Shanghai reopening, improved near-term consumption expectations. The U.S. dollar’s limited strength provided support, while copper was steady near $9,537/tonne and other metals were mixed to slightly higher. | SMM reports NPI prices may stay volatile around 1,500–1,550 yuan/mtu, tracking NPI plant costs that could fall with more nickel ore supply and lower ore prices. It says the NPI-to-nickel linkage is weaker due to a large spread since March, so NPI declines should not greatly pressure nickel in the short term. | SMM reports sharp declines in Shanghai spot nickel. Jinchuan nickel averages about 4,000 yuan/mt (down sharply), while NORNICKEL averages 5,400 yuan/mt (down 850). The drop is linked to traders shipping as the pure nickel import window opened, falling premiums, and weaker nickel briquette quotes as futures fell. | SMM reports that LME nickel inventories have fallen to a two-year low, with May 20 seeing a new low around 71,898 mt. SHFE data show nickel inventories dropped further last week, down 9.61% to 2,877 mt on May 27, a record low. | Cordoba Minerals begins feasibility-study technical work and a 40,000-metre infill drilling program at its San Matias copper-gold-silver project in Colombia. The company cites prior PFS economics using US$3.60/lb copper and notes the current spot copper price, but provides no new supply or demand changes that would move prices. Nickel/aluminium not mentioned. | Tartisan Nickel Corp. says it has started environmental baseline studies for the Kenbridge Nickel Project to support permitting and approvals. It retained consulting firms for time-sensitive Spring 2022 work and plans further updates on a summer work program. The release is a development/permitting step, not imminent nickel production.

news_summary: LME aluminium slid to a six-month low as investors feared weaker Chinese demand from COVID lockdowns, higher inflation and interest-rate hikes, and a stronger U.S. dollar. Inventories are at low levels, but macro-driven demand concerns outweighed support. Copper and nickel also declined during the sell-off. | China’s nickel sulphate output rose in May as some producers restarted and tolling increased. Demand stayed weak, but raw material costs fell, easing nickel salt prices and improving plant margins. For June-July, recovery in precursor production should lift nickel sulphate prices from lows, though abundant feed supply may cap upside. | Manitou Gold announced discovery of a nickel-bearing ultramafic complex at its Goudreau project and reported diamond drilling results including a 0.25% Ni intersection over 48 m. It also notes ultramafic intrusions along a deformation zone analogous to Crawford/Dumont nickel deposits. The release is early exploration and not production-related. | Anteros Metals updates that Phase One VTEM airborne electromagnetic surveying is underway at its Hopedale nickel project in Labrador, aiming to detect deep conductivity anomalies tied to nickel mineralization and possibly Ni-Cu-Co sulphides. The company is awaiting results to guide future surface work and potential drilling, with no production impacts stated. | Lunnon Metals reported a maiden JORC mineral resource at the Baker Shoot target in its Kambalda nickel project: 568,000 tonnes at 2.8% nickel for 15,800t contained nickel. The discovery lifts the project’s total contained nickel to 64,300t and prompts further drilling, studies, and permitting.

news_summary: SMM reports that spot premiums for pure nickel increased as the future price corrected sharply and spot supply tightened. Traders bought on dips when premiums were low, lifting pre-nickel premiums. Nickel briquette prices fell mainly because premiums narrowed, and demand/transactions were weaker versus mainstream nickel plate feedstocks.

news_summary: Fastmarkets reports China nickel sulfate prices falling and likely pressured by increased availability of feedstocks such as MHP and nickel matte, including higher imports and expanded Indonesian production. news_summary: SMM reports nickel prices falling sharply: SHFE nickel dropped for five days and spot refined #1 nickel fell about 8% since June 8 to 207,200 yuan/mt. The declines are linked to US Fed rate-hike concerns. Despite some premium strength from rare sources, demand remains slack and SMM expects nickel to stay rangebound at low levels. | SMM says Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 11,000 wmt week-on-week to 4.88 million wmt as of June 17. Ni content totals 38,000 wmt, with declines across seven major ports. The supply gap persists after Philippines weather disruptions, keeping nickel ore tight and supporting nickel demand and prices. | SMM reports SHFE nickel dropped below 200,000 yuan/mt despite a brief rebound after the Fed rate hike boosted sentiment. However, growth worries persisted and prices weakened. LEM and SHFE nickel fell on increased short selling, with demand for alloys and stainless steel still poor and supply expected to ease early next week. | Copper prices edged up as traders priced in strike risk at Chile’s Codelco, the world’s largest copper producer, alongside slightly better China activity data. news_summary: SMM reports nickel prices fell on hotter-than-expected US June inflation, raising fears of a 100 bps Fed hike, then rebounded as officials signaled a possible 75 bps hike. Spot premiums and SHFE/LME ratio improved, aiding overseas clearance optimism. Demand remained mixed, leaving spot transactions poor last week. | SMM reports on July 25 that Jinchuan nickel spot premiums fell to 13,500–14,200 yuan/mt as some pure nickel spot supply arrived. NORNICKEL premiums also declined. Nickel briquette trading was slack because futures rose, lowering briquette cost efficiency, and alloy demand remains tied to nickel plate.

news_summary: Norilsk Nickel reported 1H 2022 output: nickel nearly 100 kt (+26% y/y) but down 6% q/q to 48 kt due to Harjavalta scheduled repairs, while copper rose to 204 kt (+18% y/y) and to 113 kt in Q2 (+23% q/q) due to planned furnace maintenance. Guidance from Russian feed reiterated.

Pure nickel demand remains weak, though low inventories may offer some support. | Nornickel reports 1H2022 IFRS results, stating revenue was supported by higher nickel and copper prices and production volumes after earlier operational suspensions at mines and a concentrator. It also highlights logistical disruptions and inventory build-up. news_summary: SMM discusses nickel market drivers, citing capacity bottlenecks and oversupply that have pushed MHP, NPI, and high-grade nickel matte prices lower. Nickel briquette prices also slid, with limited transactions due to poorer cost efficiency. | CanAlaska announced winter 2022 drill assay results at its Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba, reporting high-grade nickel-sulphide mineralization in all drill holes and continuity beneath the former mine. news_summary: Indonesia’s senior minister said Tesla has signed contracts worth about $5 billion to buy battery-related materials from nickel processing companies in the country. Indonesia has been courting Tesla to invest, citing its major nickel reserves. The deals imply strong incremental nickel demand tied to battery production needs. | Indonesia’s senior cabinet minister said Tesla agreed to roughly $5 billion in five-year contracts to buy nickel battery materials from processing firms in Morowali, Sulawesi. The nickel feedstock will be used in Tesla’s lithium batteries, reinforcing near-term demand expectations for nickel. | Great Atlantic Resources reports drill results from its Mascarene Property in New Brunswick, including a 0.40 m intercept grading 6.74% copper (with gold and silver) and other copper-bearing sulfide veins. It also notes copper-nickel-cobalt occurrence context and anomalous nickel/cobalt, but no production impact. | Great Atlantic Resources reports new drill core results from its Mascarene Property in New Brunswick, including a 0.40m intercept grading 6.74% copper and anomalous nickel (plus cobalt) and gold/silver. The findings support copper-bearing veins, but the update is early exploration without production implications for metal prices. | SMM reports China’s nickel sulphate output rose in July, with battery-grade production increasing and inventories for battery-grade further declining, tightening retail supply. Due to strong MHP profits, August output is expected to rise ~4% month-on-month, mainly from high-grade nickel matte lines, suggesting easing nickel sulphate supply that can weigh on nickel prices. | Gungnir Resources reports first 2022 assays from continuing drilling at its Lappvattnet nickel deposit in Sweden, including wide zones up to 19m at 0.80% Ni and peak grades above 4%. Drilling is progressing to the eastern area and a new EM target, suggesting resource expansion potential. | Tesla reportedly signed about $5 billion in five-year nickel supply contracts with Indonesian processing companies. The nickel will be used in Tesla’s lithium batteries, according to a cabinet minister. With Indonesia the largest nickel producer and export curbs intended to support downstream supply, the deal signals stronger nickel demand. | Fastmarkets says MHP (from Indonesian laterite) is increasingly the preferred feedstock for nickel sulfate producers. Despite rapidly rising MHP output, China’s conversion capacity is still tight, keeping nickel sulfate supply constrained since late July. Producers cite rising offer prices and an MHP payable uptrend as demand picks up.

Nickel briquette prices increased mainly from narrowed premiums, but spot demand remained weak due to lower product appeal. | Alpha Copper reports results and ongoing drill progress at its Indata copper-gold project, targeting vein gold and porphyry copper-gold mineralization with associated molybdenum. It describes chalcopyrite mineralization and encourages copper-bearing intercepts, which can affect near-term copper market sentiment. No nickel or aluminium impacts are indicated. | Tesla has signed nickel-containing product supply contracts with two Indonesian companies, including a reported $5 billion deal for five years, as Indonesia positions itself as a hub for battery-related green industries. The announcement supports expectations of higher nickel demand linked to EV and battery growth.

news_summary: The piece examines China’s primary nickel supply-demand balance for Q3–Q4, noting that LME nickel has lost some value-discovery influence after the March squeeze and that China’s pricing method for imported nickel raw materials has changed. It also links nickel intermediate products’ supply and costs to battery-chemical production. | CanAlaska completed a VTEM Plus and magnetic geophysical survey at its Hunter nickel project in Manitoba’s Thompson Nickel Belt. Results highlight multiple target areas, possible additional host stratigraphy, and a modeled conductor extension trending from the Mel nickel deposit. The company plans 3D inversion and Maxwell modeling to refine drilling targets. | Canada Nickel confirmed a second significant discovery at its Deloro ultramafic target in Ontario after assay results from recent drilling. Holes DEL22-01 and DEL22-09 intersected long sections of ~0.25–0.28% nickel. The findings bolster the company’s Timmins nickel district exploration plans and complement its Crawford project permitting progress.

news_summary: China’s ferronickel imports fell to 387,400 mt in July for a second straight monthly decline, with Indonesian-origin shipments down 16% month-on-month. Meanwhile, China’s NPI inventories are in surplus, leading to production curtailments. Indonesia is also said to consider nickel export tariffs targeting NPI and ferronickel. | SMM data show China’s customs clearance volume of pure nickel fell to 9,517 mt in July, down 660 mt vs June, driven by weaker downstream demand after late-July restocking. Import profitability was pressured by rising bonded premiums and poor terminal demand, and spot imports are expected to remain loss-making in August. | China’s nickel ore imports were 4.37 million mt in July, up slightly month-on-month for two months but at the lowest level in recent years. Imports from the Philippines increased, while Indonesia and New Caledonia declined. Gains were muted by weak Chinese demand and a lacklustre market. | SMM reports that on Aug 23, Jinchuan nickel spot premiums fell to 3,500–5,500 yuan/mt (avg 4,500), down 900 from the prior day, while Nornickel premiums rose slightly. Despite futures remaining high, downstream demand stayed weak, spot transactions remained poor, and physical pricing weakened for nickel. | High temperatures triggered stronger power consumption controls and rationing across multiple regions, including Zhejiang. SMM says power curtailment severely impacted nickel sulphate producers, cutting Zhejiang nickel sulphate output from 5,388 mt Ni in July to 4,416 mt in August (down 18% m/m), with only minimal growth domestically.

news_summary: Blackstone Minerals says it has advanced its Vietnam green nickel project, completing bench tests, commissioning pilot unit operations, and converting mixed hydroxide precipitate into battery-grade nickel sulphate. The company is also progressing nickel mine pilot work and feasibility studies, which should support improving near-term sentiment for nickel processing.

news_summary: Nickel prices fell as macro sentiment worsened on hawkish Fed expectations and overseas primary nickel weakened. Fundamentals pointed to supply surplus: NPI plant inventories rose, Indonesia NPI inflows increased, and NPI prices were unlikely to rise. Demand showed only modest improvement, leaving net bearish pressure on nickel.

news_summary: Battery-grade nickel sulphate prices followed nickel slightly higher last week. SMM says insufficient MHP-based nickel sulphate capacity will be hard to resolve through the rest of the year. While high-grade matte-based capacity has grown, spot supply remains tight, and despite weak restocking demand, prices are expected to stay at highs this week. | Copper prices fell as markets turned more pessimistic about global growth, raising concerns of weaker metals demand; the weak U.S. dollar limited declines. Fitch Solutions cut its copper forecast. Aluminium also weakened on lower Chinese import volumes tied to strong domestic production, while nickel moved higher amid mixed signals. | SMM reports Ni 1.5% ore averaging $67.5/wmt CIF on Sep 16, flat on week, while low-grade FOB/CIF prices rose on scarce supply ahead of the rainy season. news_summary: SMM reports Western countries continued importing nickel from Russia despite sanctions, with US Russian nickel imports rising 70% from March to June and EU up 22%. Russia supplies about 10% of global nickel and Nornickel provides key battery-grade supply. The increased inflows suggest added near-term supply pressure on nickel prices. | A nickel mining executive says Canada has fallen behind global nickel output and processing is dominated by Indonesia and China. She argues Canada needs many new mines and refineries by 2030 to meet growing battery-driven demand. She also cites U.S. Inflation Reduction Act incentives boosting nickel sulphate eligibility. | China’s nickel ore imports fell in August to 4.34mn mt, below expectations, with laterite ore weaker and total domestic imports down 13.1% YoY. Poor stainless/NPI demand reduced smelter restocking, while weather limited flows from the Philippines and New Caledonia. Imports may rise into the rainy season restocking period.

news_summary: China’s GEM, via its QMB unit, partnered with Australia’s Nickel Industries to secure annual limonite laterite ore supply from Hengjaya’s Indonesian mine. The deal aims to back long-term production of battery materials, with yearly pricing agreements, potentially supporting near-term expectations for nickel feedstock tightness.

news_summary: Power Nickel reported its NI 43-101 technical report and mineral resource estimate for the Nisk nickel project and has started a second round of drilling (~5,500 m) to infill gaps and expand high-grade mineralization. The update is intended to grow resource confidence, with only limited copper linkage via NiEq. | SMM reports the SS contract rose sharply despite the Fed’s 75bp hike matching expectations. It attributes part of the move to strong SHFE nickel gains earlier in the week. Fundamentals for stainless steel show only slight changes in supply and demand, with holiday activity supporting short-term prices. | Surge Battery Metals announced completion of Phase 1 exploration on its Ni100 nickel project in northern British Columbia, collecting rock and soil samples targeting ultramafic anomalies. Many samples returned over 2,000 ppm nickel, with ICP results pending and further work (including potential drilling) planned. | Power Nickel announced a NI 43-101 compliant mineral resource estimate for its Nisk nickel-copper project and has begun a second drilling round (~5,500 m) to infill and expand higher-grade zones. The disclosure may affect market expectations for future nickel/copper supply but not near-term production.

news_summary: A weekly broker-rating roundup highlights Citi’s view of short-term headwinds for base-metals demand from sluggish China, higher US interest rates and a stronger USD. Citi trims near-term price forecasts for aluminium, copper and nickel (while being comparatively more bearish on copper). Some company ratings are upgraded on valuation, but metal-price expectations lean weaker. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty from China’s continued zero-COVID curbs and global recession fears offset worries over tight supply. LME and Shanghai copper prices gave back gains. Aluminium and nickel also declined as risk sentiment weakened, despite lower Chile output and tight inventories. | Copper rose on tightening global supplies: LME/COMEX/ShFE stocks fell and Chilean production dropped sharply. China demand improved at the margin via premiums, but gains were capped by ongoing zero-COVID uncertainty and global recession fears. Aluminium and nickel fell in the same session, showing mixed risk appetite. | SMM reports SHFE nickel stayed rangebound, with upside pressure from strong downstream pure nickel demand and occasional drops in stainless steel prices. US CPI and core CPI exceeding expectations weighed on nickel. Chinese refined output was slightly lower MoM, and near-term price outlook for SHFE 2211 is 172,000–192,000 yuan/mt. | SMM estimates China’s nickel ore port arrivals rose to 1.27m wmt (Sep 30 week) and 1.69m wmt (Oct 7 week). With the Philippines rainy season approaching, early-purchased ore is arriving now, keeping imports high. Increased NPI demand helped stabilize and lift nickel ore prices. | Copper prices were subdued as demand uncertainty outweighed supply woes. LME copper gave up gains and hovered around $7,535/t while inventories fell and Chile production dropped sharply. China’s reiterated zero-COVID stance and recession fears limited upside, with aluminium and nickel also slipping. | Element 29 Resources reported its first resource estimate for the Elida copper project in Peru, with inferred tonnes grading 0.32% copper and small molybdenum credits, and plans further drilling and metallurgical studies. It also plans to update resources at Flor de Cobre. Overall, it’s copper-positive for sentiment, not a direct near-term supply driver.

news_summary: SMM reports spot pure nickel supply turned scarce again on Oct 19, lifting NORNICKEL premiums to about 5,000 yuan/mt (+200). Jinchuan premiums averaged 10,250 yuan/mt (flat). Nickel briquette prices rose, but liquidity weakened. Supply may improve after SHFE 2210 contract goods are released. | Copper prices fell further as soaring inflation, recession worries and expectations of more rate hikes pressured global demand sentiment, while a stronger dollar weighed on metal prices. LME aluminium also slipped amid additional inflows into approved warehouses and rising inventories. Nickel rose slightly, but the overall tone remains bearish. | North Dakota’s Mercer County will host a $433 million Talon Nickel ore processing and management facility, backed by a $114 million U.S. federal cost-share grant. The plant will process ore from Talon’s Tamarack mine for battery minerals. The policy aims to strengthen domestic supply chains, reducing reliance on foreign nickel sources. | Talon Metals announced a near-doubling of indicated resources at its Tamarack Nickel Project, including a 570% rise in high-grade massive sulphide contained nickel. The update strengthens expectations for future nickel concentrate supply potential from the Tamarack nickel-copper deposit, with limited secondary implications for copper. | North Dakota’s Mercer County will host Talon Nickel’s $433 million ore processing and management facility, funded partly by a $114 million U.S. grant. The plant will process nickel ore from Talon’s Tamarack mine to support U.S. EV and grid battery supply chains, creating about 150 jobs. | Talon Nickel will build a $433 million ore processing and management facility in Mercer County, North Dakota, funded in part by $114 million from the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law. The plant will process raw ore from Talon’s Tamarack nickel mine to support domestic battery mineral supply for EVs and the power grid.

news_summary: Nearctic Nickel Mines Inc. says its Quebec affiliate, Ungava Mineral Exploration, received a Statement of Defense in an ongoing Quebec Ministry of Energy and Natural Resources action. The company notes amendments to a Motion for Damages filed in 2015 and updated in May 2022. No operational or production changes are described.

news_summary: SMM reports Chinese nickel ore port inventories rose 328,000 wmt WoW to 8.26 million wmt as NPI plants restocked before the Philippines rainy season. Total nickel content was 65,000 mt. With rainy-season conditions in southern Philippines expected to lift inventories short term, ore prices get some support. | Recharge Resources plans to spin out its 100% owned Pinchi Lake nickel project in Canada into a new subsidiary, NextCharge Battery Metals Corp., subject to conditions and regulatory approval. The announcement is procedural and does not change near-term output or supply, implying limited immediate impact on nickel prices.

news_summary: First Quantum Minerals says it will apply for new exploration licenses in Zambia late 2022 or early 2023, citing improved business conditions under President Hakainde Hichilema after years of stagnant copper output. The planned exploration targets copper and nickel, aimed at easing global shortages, though timing limits near-term price effects. | SMM reports that on Oct 24 spot premiums for pure nickel were sharply lower: Jinchuan nickel premiums fell to 8,000–8,500 yuan/mt (down 400). NORNICKEL premiums also declined (down 500). Month-end shipping activity and weaker spot transactions followed prior futures-driven premium spikes. | As of Oct 21, nickel ore arrivals at Chinese ports were 752,000 wmt, down 34% week-on-week. Shipments from the Philippines (Surigao) were constrained by rainy-season weather restrictions. With NPI prices supported by tight short-term supply, ports are likely to see smaller imports going forward, tightening balances. | SHFE nickel prices fell after an earlier rise. Pandemic-related transport restrictions delayed spot pure nickel arrivals. NPI shipments in October were steady and inventories eased, while Indonesia NPI supply was tight with high prices. Despite rising stainless steel demand, nickel is expected to trade volatile this week. | SMM reports September 2022 China nickel ore imports rose, but port inventory is only slightly higher and the market remains tight. With the Philippines rainy season approaching, nickel ore shipments face weather restrictions, likely reducing laterite nickel ore imports. The article expects imports to trend downward during the rainy season. | Rusal filed a London lawsuit alleging Norilsk Nickel CEO Vladimir Potanin breached a decade-old shareholder pact that was meant to protect Nornickel’s dividend formula. The dispute renews tension between Rusal and Potanin-controlled Interros, with the agreement set to expire and merger talks postponed. | EV Nickel reported initial assay results from four holes of Phase 3 drilling at its Shaw Dome CarLang A Zone, confirming thick dunite-hosted, large-scale nickel mineralization (e.g., 297 m at 0.25% Ni and 201 m at 0.22% Ni). Remaining assays are pending and expected over coming weeks.

news_summary: SMM says spot Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 8,250 yuan/mt, staying flat, while NORNICKEL premiums fell. Although pure nickel premiums continue to drop, spot Jinchuan supply is generally tight in October and the premium decline is limited due to severe pandemic conditions. Nickel briquette prices rose slightly, but trades were weak. | Falcon Gold expands its HSP South project by staking additional claims, increasing its land position by 70% in Havre St. Pierre’s anorthosite complex. The area is described as newly recognized nickel source and contiguous to Go Metals’ recent nickel-and-copper sulphide discovery, with airborne surveys planned. | Falcon Gold reported a 70% increase in its land position at the HSP South property in Quebec, adjacent to Go Metals’ drilling and discovery of wide intervals of nickel and copper sulphides. Falcon plans airborne EM surveys and expanded staking across prospective contact, potentially boosting near-term sentiment for nickel and copper. | SMM cites customs data showing China’s clearance volume of pure/nickel-related products rose about 11% MoM in September while remaining low YoY. Imports of nickel briquettes and powder jumped MoM, attributed to higher nickel sulphate demand for ternary precursors. Output and bonded inventories should stay supportive into October. | Nickel 28 reported Q3 2022 operating performance from its Ramu nickel-cobalt JV in Papua New Guinea, including higher contained nickel production and substantially higher nickel sales. It discusses earthquake-related curtailment and cash-cost impacts from input commodity pricing that is reportedly easing, which affects short-term nickel sentiment. | Talon Metals plans to move components of its proposed Tamarack, Minnesota nickel processing plant to North Dakota to address environmental and tribal concerns. The company says this reduces land disturbance and permitting scope. The project remains focused on nickel and has a $114 million DOE grant, but no production change is specified. | Battery OEMs are facing rising nickel costs as supply terms have shifted from fixed long-term off-takes to spot pricing for nickel salts. The piece cites nickel-salt premiums over LME nickel and a move toward lower-grade intermediates, increasing input cost volatility and exposure for manufacturers. | Nickel North Exploration reports 2022 sampling and backpack drilling confirming high-grade, up to 400m-long Ni-Cu sulfide mineralization at the Hawk Ridge Project’s Gamma Zone in Northern Quebec. Reported intercepts and outcrop/backpack results include strong nickel and copper grades, supporting further 2023 drilling plans.

news_summary: Cyprium Metals’ reverse-circulation drilling at the Nanadie Well project in Western Australia intersected broad anomalous copper-nickel-PGE zones, including multiple strong copper intervals and sections with meaningful nickel. The company plans follow-up work to test an expanded 1.6 km corridor, improving sentiment for future supply. | SMM reports that on Oct 26 Jinchuan nickel premium fell to 8,000 yuan/mt (down 250) and NORNICKEL premium to around 2,400 yuan/mt (down 500). Despite active near-month SHFE contract shifting, spot premiums declined, while thin spot trading and higher nickel briquette prices weakened deal activity. | SMM reports resilient Jinchuan nickel spot premiums in October amid supply tightness, with imports pressured by a weaker SHFE/LME ratio. Nickel demand remains supported by downstream stainless steel, and low inventory is cited. For NPI, demand is robust and the market expects slight short-term upward room, unless Indonesian flows surge. | SMM reports SHFE nickel rising for a third consecutive day (+1.35%) with spot refined nickel up to about 195,050 yuan/mt. Tight Jinchuan supply, limited premium declines, weaker import economics, relatively strong NPI demand, low inventories, and supportive stainless-steel demand should limit downside. | Clean Air Metals reported infill drill results at its Thunder Bay North PGE-Cu-Ni project, including copper (0.61%) and nickel (0.43%) in a 10m interval, and said it is advancing prefeasibility engineering and metallurgical testing. The update is exploration-focused with no immediate production or supply disruption signals. | Clean Air Metals reported 2022 drill results from its Thunder Bay North Current deposit, including 10.0m grading 0.61% copper and 0.43% nickel, and said it is advancing a prefeasibility study with metallurgical optimization and resource validation. The update may support longer-term supply expectations, but no immediate production impact is cited.

news_summary: Talon Metals is advancing the Tamarack nickel-copper-cobalt project in Minnesota to supply nickel for electric-vehicle batteries, citing test results and a Tesla supply deal. Local groups raise concerns about wetlands and potential runoff, but the company says processing occurs out of state. The prospect supports nickel sentiment more than near-term supply.

news_summary: SMM reports SHFE nickel fell in early trading. In the spot market on Oct 28, Jinchuan nickel premiums averaged 6,250 yuan/mt, down 400 yuan/mt, while NORNICKEL premiums averaged 1,550 yuan/mt, down 350 yuan/mt. Spot supply rose and shipments increased; nickel briquette prices also dropped as demand remained weak. | SMM said China’s bonded zone nickel inventory fell by 700 mt to 3,900 mt in the week to Oct 28. Nickel briquette stocks were 1,050 mt and nickel plate 2,850 mt. Spot pure nickel imports weakened, but some briquettes cleared customs on demand from nickel sulphate producers despite off-season plate demand. | SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports rose 289,000 wmt to 8.55 million wmt (67,000 mt Ni content) as October purchases before the Philippine rainy season arrived. Port stocks are expected to keep increasing short term, though growth may be capped by rainy-season disruptions in the Philippines and later decline.

news_summary: Copper prices edged lower as bleak near-term manufacturing and metals demand outweighed hopes that China may ease COVID-19 controls next year. Global risk sentiment weakened ahead of a large U.S. rate hike, likely stifling activity. Tight supply and low inventories were cited as support, leaving overall bias mixed. | Base metals prices rose on Tuesday after widespread rumors that China might change/ease its zero-Covid policy. Three-month futures gained for copper (+2.7%), aluminium (+0.9%), and nickel (+8.2%). Analysts said reopening hopes lifted China risk assets and triggered short covering; nickel additionally benefited from strong EV sales.

news_summary: Copper prices fell as markets digested a hawkish Fed stance signaling prolonged policy tightening, weakening risk sentiment and near-term demand expectations. China’s elevated COVID cases added pressure on industrial and construction activity. The report also notes lower tin and lead, with aluminium dipping and nickel edging up.

Follow-up drilling aims to confirm continuity of the nickel-bearing unit. | Participants at Antaike’s nickel and cobalt conference highlighted uncertainty for 2023 but noted bullish pressure in Chinese nickel sulfate markets. SHFE deliverable-brand decisions and LME Russia-material guidance add volatility.

The incident raises short-term supply concerns by disrupting mining operations and potential remediation and regulatory response, which can tighten nickel availability expectations over the coming days and pressure nickel prices lower. | Canada Nickel reported successful pilot plant testing at its Crawford nickel project, showing improved iron and chromium recoveries and higher iron grade in magnetite concentrate, plus nickel recovery to the magnetic concentrate. news_summary: The headline reports that SK On plans to build a nickel production facility in Indonesia in partnership with EcoPro and China’s GEM. The announcement relates to future nickel supply capacity and could modestly lift market expectations for nickel availability, supporting prices in the short term while production timing remains uncertain. | Los Cerros acquired nickel-copper projects in Papua New Guinea from Footprint Resources and plans diamond drilling and geophysical work. Historical results include a trench assay with 2.6% copper and float nickel sulphide up to 45.8%. The move is exploratory and meant to attract JV partners, not to signal near-term production changes. | Los Cerros acquired five nickel-copper projects in Papua New Guinea via Footprint Resources and plans a 3,000m drill program at Kusi and geophysical work at Veri Veri, targeting high-grade nickel sulphide and historical copper-bearing assays. The news boosts exploration activity but offers no immediate supply impact for benchmark copper or nickel prices. | SK On will build a nickel MHP facility in Indonesia with EcoPro and GEM, aiming for 30,000 tons of high-purity nickel starting Q3 2024. MHP is used to produce nickel sulfate and EV battery precursors. The project also seeks oxidized nickel ore supply, potentially supportive for nickel. | Technology Minerals reports grab-sample assay results from its Aramo project in Asturias, Spain, including up to 28% copper, 1.88% cobalt, and 1.68% nickel. The company completed a 3D laser survey for underground mapping. The news is exploration-positive, potentially improving short-term sentiment for copper and nickel. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore inventories fell by 98,000 wmt WoW to 8.94 million wmt as of Nov 25, with port inventory down to 4.68 million wmt. Low supply and reduced shipments persist, while NPI plants buy less due to lower NPI prices. Gradual further declines are expected. | GEM, EcoPro, and SK On will jointly build and operate an HPAL nickel plant in Indonesia’s Morowali. The facility targets 30,000 tonnes of nickel per year from mixed hydroxide precipitate. It aims to secure stable nickel feedstock and meet US IRA sourcing requirements, supporting market confidence in nickel supply. | Lygend Resources & Technology plans a HK IPO raising about $470m, using 56% of proceeds to develop its Indonesia HPAL nickel hydrometallurgy project. The project targets large nickel-cobalt compound capacity and has prior phase commissioning, with full lines expected by Q4 2023, potentially supporting nickel production outlook.

news_summary: SMM says nickel ore inventories at Chinese ports fell 23,000 wmt WoW to 8.92 million wmt, with Philippines shipment outflows declining and inflows slowing. Meanwhile, NPI plants faced losses from weaker NPI prices and steel mills cut output, reducing ore demand. Stocks may decline only modestly short term, with imports elsewhere still sizable. | Poseidon Nickel Ltd said it is fully funded and progressing toward a decision to proceed with its Black Swan nickel project. While it does not indicate immediate production, moving the project forward can increase expectations of future nickel supply, which may weigh on nickel prices over the coming days to weeks.

news_summary: Copper prices gained as China began easing COVID-19 curbs, boosting hopes for demand recovery in the top consumer. LME three-month copper rose 0.5% and Shanghai January copper edged slightly lower. Gains were limited by signs of softer imported copper demand and slowing inventory withdrawals, while the dollar stayed steady.

Goldman lifted its copper price forecast and cited tightening visible stocks. Non-ferrous prices broadly moved higher, with aluminium and nickel also up, supported by a weaker dollar. | SMM reports Dec 9 bonded-zone nickel inventories rising to 6,300 mt, including 1,370 mt of briquettes and 4,930 mt of nickel plate. With weak customs clearance and rigid domestic demand, some nickel was redirected to ports. | SMM said Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 29,000 wmt to 8.89 million wmt as the Philippines remains in rainy season and port stock declines. news_summary: Copper prices fell from near six-month highs as the U.S. dollar firmed on stronger-than-expected U.S. producer inflation, raising concerns about higher-for-longer Fed rates. Markets also turned cautious ahead of central bank meetings. LME aluminium and SHFE nickel also dropped alongside copper. | Copper fell on the LME and Comex as concerns grew that China’s economy will weaken due to spreading COVID cases and ongoing factory curbs. Uncertainty over whether the Fed and other central banks will slow rate hikes added pressure. The same macro concerns weighed on aluminium and nickel. | Indonesia has appealed a WTO ruling that backed the EU in a dispute over Jakarta’s ban on nickel ore exports and related domestic processing requirements. The EU argued Indonesia’s restrictions harmed its stainless-steel industry, while Indonesia banned nickel ore exports from early 2020. The appeal could prolong uncertainty over supply restrictions.

However, surging COVID-19 infections raise concerns about manufacturing disruption. SHFE January copper fell. news_summary: SMM reports that mid- and high-grade nickel ore prices rose slightly in November on low arrivals and scarce supply, partly offset by lower ocean freight. However, poor demand and falling NPI prices have weakened momentum. For December, SMM expects nickel ore prices to fall slightly, with Ni 1.5% ranging $70–75/wmt.

news_summary: SMM reports that in November nickel sulphate supply was in a small surplus despite sluggish downstream demand. A battery-grade deficit turned into a surplus due to weaker demand and delayed downstream purchases alongside ternary producers dumping inventories. For December, precursor output is set to fall and nickel salt output only slightly, leaving a surplus that pressures prices. | Canada Nickel says it has filed the Detailed Project Description and related responses to advance the federal impact assessment process for its Crawford nickel sulphide project, following prior permitting steps and consultations. The company also hired Deutsche Bank and Scotiabank to advise on equity financing alternatives, targeting permits by mid-2025. | SHFE nickel prices continued to decline, with spot refined nickel and briquettes lower as demand remained weak. Inventories rose in some areas and LME stocks increased. While macro improved modestly after weaker-than-expected US CPI, downstream stainless steel and alloy consumption stayed soft, limiting upside. | KoBold Metals plans a $150m investment in Zambia’s Mingomba copper-cobalt project, including a $115m payment tied to stakes and $35m for exploration. It aims to develop the high-grade deposit using AI-enabled drilling analysis. Zambia targets much higher copper output by 2032, but near-term production impact is unclear. | The report’s headline says the U.S. sanctioned Russia’s richest tycoon and Nornickel chief Potanin. It highlights the Nickel Shäw Project in Yukon, recent drilling intersecting near-surface massive/semi-massive nickel-copper sulphides, and completed studies toward saleable nickel/copper concentrate. The company frames the move as providing near-term smaller scale opportunities, which can modestly improve near-term nickel supply expectations. | SMM reports that spot pure nickel supply remained tight on Dec 20, pushing Jinchuan premiums to an average of 10,250 yuan/mt (+1,900) and NORNICKEL premiums to 7,750 yuan/mt (+1,300). Despite rigid demand, transactions stayed thin, reinforcing bullish short-term nickel tightness. | LME data showed nickel inventories increased to 54,360 mt, a three-and-a-half-month high after a prior decline. SHFE nickel inventories fell sharply on Dec 16 but overall fluctuated. The piece notes that declining inventories usually support futures, implying the LME build is a short-term bearish signal for nickel. | Canada Nickel agreed to acquire the past-producing Texmont mine in Canada for cash and a promissory note, with the option to restart operations. The company is drilling to update resources and assess whether output could support a restart by 2025 or provide higher-grade feed for its Crawford nickel project, lifting nickel sentiment. | Russian miner Nornickel is considering a 10% output cut next year. Because Nornickel is a key nickel producer, the prospect of lower supply can tighten expected market balances and lift nickel price sentiment within weeks as traders price in reduced future availability.

news_summary: Clive Palmer has sold Queensland’s Yabulu nickel and cobalt refinery to Swiss-based Zero Carbon Investek for an estimated $1–2 billion. The buyer plans to restart operations, aiming to employ hundreds and later add solar and battery power to decarbonize production. Sale is subject to regulatory approvals. | SMM reports China imported about 14,521 mt of refined nickel in Nov 2022, but customs clearance volumes for refined/pure nickel fell 13% month-on-month. The decline is linked to nickel import losses after the SHFE/LME ratio dropped, and rising bonded inventories. SMM expects further clearance decreases in December. | Premium Nickel Resources announced additional assay results from its Selebi Ni-Cu-Co sulphide mine in Botswana, including multiple drillhole intervals with reported nickel and copper grades and NiEq calculations based on LME prices. The update is exploratory/resource-expansion oriented and does not change near-term production or inventories, limiting short-term price impact. | Clive Palmer’s Queensland Nickel/Yabulu refinery was sold for an estimated $1–$2 billion to Zero Carbon Investek, with plans to restart operations and employ hundreds. The deal is framed as happening in more favourable conditions for nickel tied to battery demand, supporting near-term nickel sentiment.

news_summary: SMM expects SHFE nickel to remain supported as pure nickel supply stays tight, keeping spot premiums high and Indonesian NPI nearly fully booked. Downstream demand for stainless steel/alloys is only slightly improving, and some restocking occurs ahead of Chinese New Year, supporting a bullish tone for nickel in the near term. | SMM reports Dec 27 spot nickel premiums: Jinchuan at about 8,500 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL around 7,150 yuan/mt, both lower than the prior day. Premiums eased as traders became more willing to ship late month, but spot transactions were thin because high futures prices and weak briquette liquidity limited buying. | Nickel 28’s chairman provides a year-end update tied to its Ramu Nickel operation and nickel sulphide royalties. It discusses 2022 nickel market dynamics, higher sulfur costs, expected nickel/cobalt production, and that debt repayment timing depends on nickel prices. It also references progress at Dumont and Turnagain projects.

Workers protested over safety and pay demands. The first phase of GNI is planned to reach production with multiple lines, but the accident implies short-term output losses. | The article argues nickel is likely to stay oversupplied through 2026 due to strong Indonesian output and weaker demand for stainless steel and nickel batteries. Spot transactions are thin and Jinchuan and NORNICKEL nickel premiums fell. While NPI supply is relatively tight and SS futures rose, pure nickel demand cannot support prices. | SMM reviews 2022 nickel volatility and outlines 2023 risks. It cites falling LME inventories early in the year, later demand weakness, tight spot supply from a closed import window, and then expects a significant refined nickel and overall primary nickel surplus. Rising NPI supply and pessimistic macro could keep nickel prices downward. | SMM reports spot premiums for Jinchuan nickel fell to 7,000–8,000 yuan/mt, with the average down 1,000 yuan/mt day-on-day. news_summary: SMM reports that nickel-salt and nickel sulphate producers faced limited profits because high-cost nickel inputs and still-heavy high-priced inventories weighed on margins. Nickel sulphate firms stabilized some costs, but lower nickel sulphate prices cut profits further, while downstream ternary precursor buyers reduced purchase prices amid weak demand, leaving the market stalled. | GEM says the first phase of Qingmeibang Indonesia’s nickel resources project (30,000 mt Ni content annual capacity) has started operations. The project is ramping up and is expected to produce over 20,000 mt of nickel content in 2023. The update signals additional nickel supply entering the market.

news_summary: SMM reports that imported NORNICKEL nickel losses expanded as LME nickel prices rose. Spot premiums for Jinchuan nickel fell to an average of 6,000 yuan/mt and NORNICKEL nickel to about 6,750 yuan/mt, both down sharply day-on-day. Nickel briquette trading stayed weak due to relative pricing versus nickel sulphate. | SMM reports nickel prices surged again, with SHFE and LME nickel hitting highs amid very low LME warrants and weak liquidity, driving speculation and extreme volatility. It warns that nickel-linked pricing for intermediates is becoming harder to trade. Nickel sulphate demand is weak and a surplus is expected in 2023. | Metal Energy reports completion of a 10,000-metre drill program and acquisition of full (100%) interest in the Manibridge nickel project in Manitoba. Drill holes intersected visible nickel sulphides, with some assay highlights and remaining results pending, supporting future development potential. It cites high recent nickel demand amid EV and storage growth. | Copper fell to two-month lows as traders prioritized demand fears from slowing global growth and rising COVID cases in China, outweighing dollar weakness. Aluminium also dropped on sliding energy prices that raise smelting economics. Nickel plunged alongside the broader base-metals sell-off. | Antam said its Haltim Ferronickel Plant in East Halmahera will start operating in the second semester of 2023 after power and construction phases progress. The plant’s annual capacity is 13,500 tonnes, lifting Antam’s ferronickel capacity to 40,500 tonnes per year, potentially increasing nickel supply expectations. | Global Commodities Holdings will launch a nickel trade platform with a nickel index intended to underpin future contracts competing with the LME. The initiative follows last March’s chaotic LME nickel trading suspension and seeks direct physical-market trading, potentially improving price discovery and sentiment for nickel in the coming weeks.

news_summary: SHFE nickel prices fell for a second consecutive day, dropping about 5% in the session, as Tsingshan-related nickel matte supply grows and macro sentiment turns pessimistic on expectations of a Fed interest rate hike. Inventories abroad rose, while tightness in domestic stocks eased, keeping nickel weak near term. | Magna Mining reported additional drill results from the Crean Hill 101 Footwall Zone, including 31.1m at 4.0% Ni and also notable associated Cu grades. The company says results show continuity of high-grade massive sulphide mineralization and expects further assays. This is exploration-focused, not production-related. | Green River Gold Corp. reports additional assay and drilling results from the Quesnel Nickel Project, noting consistent nickel mineralization at depth at the Deep Purple Zone 2 target. Six more winter drill holes are planned, but the announcement is exploration-stage and does not indicate immediate production impacts for nickel.

news_summary: Copper prices rose as expectations grow that China will support its ailing property sector, improving demand outlook and helping inventory restocking. LME copper was up 1.2%. Nickel fell sharply to a one-month low on supply expectations after Tsingshan’s new electronickel project started production. Aluminium also edged higher. | Copper prices rose on hopes China will add property-sector support measures, improving the near-term demand outlook. LME three-month copper and SHFE February copper both increased, while Chile production fell. In contrast, Shanghai nickel fell on supply expectations from Tsingshan’s new project starting production. Aluminium also edged up. | SMM says nickel ore inventories across Chinese ports fell 370,000 wmt to 8.81 million wmt as of Jan 6, with Ni content down to about 70,000 mt. Supply tightness from poor Philippines weather is cited, and further declines are expected, with demand possibly improving after Chinese New Year. | Copper rose to a three-week high on a weaker U.S. dollar and optimism that China will boost property demand. LME three-month copper climbed about 1.2%. The piece also references Chile output declines supporting copper, while nickel weakness reflects Tsingshan project-related supply expectations and aluminium advanced.

news_summary: SMM reviews 2022 nickel price volatility tied to Russia-Ukraine risks, later COVID/macro effects, and changing supply-demand fundamentals. It notes lower LME/SHFE inventories but still highlights rising output and expected primary nickel surplus in 2023, mainly from oversupply of NPI, implying nickel prices likely fall overall. | Canada’s Impact Assessment Agency will require a full federal impact assessment for Canada Nickel’s Crawford Project. The review covers Indigenous and public comments and resumes a 180-day planning phase. The company highlights potential net-zero carbon capture, but the added regulatory steps introduce timeline uncertainty for nickel supply.

news_summary: An independent Oliver Wyman review says the LME lacked sufficient tools to curb the short-term distortions during March’s nickel price “chaos.” It urges the exchange to strengthen rules and oversight. The review follows the LME’s week-long nickel trading suspension and the cancellation of large volumes of trades. | SMM reports that pure nickel spot lagged futures in December but social inventories fell sharply as spot cargoes moved to LME warehouses. In January, LME prices drop amid macro/news factors, while imports remain constrained, output rises, and alloy demand restocks ahead of Chinese New Year. Overall, nickel is expected to trade rangebound. | SMM reports battery-grade nickel sulphate prices continued falling in late 2022 due to reduced NEV precursor demand after subsidy cancellation, sharp precursor production cuts, and heavy inventory selling by integrated plants. With demand set to trough end-January and output reductions planned, prices are expected to keep easing in January. | SMM reports SHFE nickel fell 11.2% entering January, after December gains. The decline is linked to macro recession worries from Fed remarks, and a shift on the news front as Tsingshan’s electrodeposited/refined nickel commissioning adds supply and weighs on deliverable conditions, alongside rising global visible nickel inventories. | NiCAN says it will begin drilling at the Wine nickel property in Manitoba, Canada. The announcement is exploratory in nature, but it can slightly lift market expectations for future nickel resources and potential supply development. Near-term price impact is likely modest since drilling is not equivalent to production. | Golden Arrow commenced an IP-resistivity survey and plans its first diamond drilling program in Q1 at the San Pietro Cu-Au-Co project in Chile. The company aims to refine drill targets for copper and cobalt using geophysics and prior drilling data. It is exploration activity with no immediate production impact. | Nickel prices surged above $30,000/t in mid-November but fell sharply the next day amid low liquidity and heightened market monitoring by the LME. It also flags supply-risk events (Goro tailings leak, Indonesian plant rumour) and ongoing Russia-related uncertainty, reinforcing short-term volatility and bearish price pressure. | Britain’s FCA is blocking the LME’s plan to restart nickel trading in Asian hours, saying supervisory controls are inadequate while London is asleep. The LME previously suspended nickel activity for eight days in March 2022 after a spike. Ongoing inactivity has hurt liquidity and volumes, pressuring confidence. | Britain’s FCA is blocking the LME from restarting nickel trading in Asian hours, citing doubts over supervisory controls and orderly-market management. The LME suspended nickel activity in 2022 after sharp Asian-hour price spikes. Continued delay sustains low volumes and reduced arbitrage versus ShFE, weighing on nickel sentiment.

news_summary: China’s faster-than-expected reopening is described as a “game-changer” for commodity markets, driving bullish repositioning and lifting raw-material prices. Softer US inflation reduces pressure for aggressive Fed tightening, weakening the dollar and supporting expectations for global growth, which underpins rallies including copper and likely industrial metals demand. | LME expects to resume nickel trading in Asian hours but the FCA says supervisory controls are not yet adequate, requiring tighter monitoring to contain volatility. Past nickel disruptions stemmed from low LME inventories and deliverability/ warrant issues. Meanwhile, falling LME nickel and improving supply suggest price risks are limited near term. | The LME published the final Oliver Wyman report on last year’s nickel trading scandal, citing accumulated short positions as a key factor in the March suspension. UK regulators questioned the LME’s ability to carry out plans to restart Asian trading hours. The restart reportedly faced delays amid ongoing nickel chaos.

news_summary: Talon Metals says it has identified a new nickel and copper mineralization zone about 3.2 km from its Tamarack project in central Minnesota. The discovery is expected to boost sentiment around potential future output. Near-term effects are likely limited pending further drilling, assays, and resource definition. | Talon Metals says it has discovered a new system of nickel and copper mineralization about 3.2 km from its Tamarack project in central Minnesota. The company describes the new zone’s location and potential significance, but the announcement is not tied to immediate production changes or inventories. | SMM reports SHFE nickel prices fell despite China’s GDP growth being at a normal level. Markets focus on upcoming US core PCE data and potential Fed tightening, which could strengthen the dollar and pressure commodities. Fundamentals show weak import clearance, weaker spot transactions, and tight deliverable refined nickel, suggesting limited near-term support. | SHFE nickel prices fell this week despite China’s 2022 GDP growth normalizing. Market support was limited as upstream sellers offered less and NPI producers kept low finished-product inventories. Downstream demand stayed soft due to CNY holiday rest. Ahead, potential US Fed tightening risks remain bearish for commodity prices. Nickel expected to drop further pre-CNY. | SMM says Chinese port nickel ore inventories fell 96,000 wmt WoW to 8.58 million wmt, with Ni content down 1,000 mt to about 67,000 mt. Arrivals were pressured by rainy conditions in the Philippines, while NPI and steel mills suspended output for overhauls. Inventory may decline faster after Chinese New Year.

news_summary: Nickel premiums fell across the US, with nickel briquette and 4x4 cathode premiums dropping after China returns from Lunar New Year. Reports show demand improving but price opinions easing, leading to lower assessed ranges. In Europe, briquette premiums decreased on weak stainless demand, while uncut cathode premiums rose slightly amid tight high-purity supply.

news_summary: Vale SA said Q4 2022 nickel production fell 1.3% to 47,400 tonnes, while nickel sales volumes jumped 30.2% to 58,200 tonnes by drawing on pent-up stocks to meet contracts. The company also noted weaker iron ore output and ongoing expectations for 2023 nickel production. | The Philippines is considering taxing exported nickel ore to attract investment in domestic nickel processing. Analysts warn tariffs would increase supply uncertainty and raise Chinese smelters’ nickel-ore procurement costs (Philippines ore is largely used). While potential future smelting capacity could expand, the near-term effect is cost pressure for primary nickel. | SMM reports Jinchuan Group boosted refined nickel production by 600 mt in January. The plan targets 6,000 mt higher refined nickel output in 2023 and relies on capacity and balance improvements across nickel smelters, using high-grade nickel matte as feedstock, with most incremental output from existing lines. | SMM reports SHFE nickel prices rising on Feb 1 while spot trade activity is slack due to high spot levels. Jinchuan and Nornickel spot premiums fell day-on-day as suppliers were keen to sell. Nickel briquette spot prices edged higher, but nickel sulphate production economics remained unattractive, limiting demand. | Atlantic Nickel reports record 2022 performance at its Santa Rita nickel-copper sulphide mine in Brazil, including higher concentrate output and details of hedging for 2022-2023. Realized prices were about US$12.23/lb Ni and US$3.70/lb Cu, but the release is mainly historical, limiting immediate price pressure. | Glencore said it will keep its 2023 output guidance despite a 12% copper production plunge in 2022, citing issues at Katanga and disruptions including an Antapaccay suspension after protests. Nickel production rose 5% on improved operations, but overall guidance stayed unchanged, limiting near-term price impact.

news_summary: Indonesia’s nickel-rich profile and continued nickel ore export ban are driving large EV and battery investment deals, with Indonesia expected to capture a major share of global nickel demand growth for batteries. It also signals upcoming export bans on copper ore and bauxite, supporting near-term expectations for tighter supplies. | The article details Indonesia’s accelerating nickel-related investment and processing chain for EV batteries, including the first shipment of nickel mixed hydroxide precipitate to China from Morowali and multiple smelter/HPAL and battery-material projects. It also notes broader EV investment deals, implying improved nickel feedstock and supply expectations. | Copper prices declined as a firm U.S. dollar following a strong jobs report made dollar-priced commodities more expensive. The move was reinforced by doubts about China’s recovery pace after COVID restrictions were removed. Aluminium and nickel also fell on their respective exchanges amid the same macro and demand pressures. | Indonesia’s export ban on nickel ore and push to build EV battery supply chains underpin expectations for rapidly rising nickel demand and sustained market prominence. Integrated firms are restocking amid concerns over future raw-material supply. | ANZ forecasts nickel will face a large supply surplus of about 55,000 mt in 2023. However, high nickel prices suppress downstream demand, with weak spot transactions, fewer shipments, and limited buying by electroplating and battery-related units. Fundamentals seen as offering weaker support going forward. | Indonesia, the world’s top nickel producer, is pushing EV battery investment and leveraging its 2020 nickel ore export ban to expand onshore processing. With EV demand rising, Indonesia is projected to add a large share of global nickel growth, tightening expectations for battery-grade nickel prices; nickel also faces potential sustainability-related constraints. | Indonesia is courting Tesla and other automakers to invest in its nickel-driven EV battery supply chain. It cites over $15bn in battery-material and EV production deals, including nickel export bans, HPAL refining plans, and nickel sulfate/MHP smelter and refining investments. These developments bolster nickel demand expectations short term. | Palladium One reported expanded drill intercepts at the West Pickle massive sulfide zone on the Tyko nickel–copper project in Ontario, extending mineralization to over 600 meters. With planned annual capacity of 40,000 mt in nickel content, the project is expected to be completed and commissioned in Q4 this year, increasing expected nickel supply. | SMM reports SHFE nickel traded lower early, but downstream restocking lifted spot transactions and inquiries. Pure nickel premiums were mostly flat and briquette trading was sluggish. | SMM reports Feb 7 pure nickel spot premiums for Jinchuan rising to 7,300–7,500 yuan/mt (+400). Nornickel premiums eased to 6,500 yuan/mt (-50). SHFE nickel opened lower but rebounded, while spot prices were slightly down and inquiries increased, supporting nickel sentiment near term. | London copper rebounded after a four-week low as the dollar weakened. Copper also faced potential near-term supply tightness after First Quantum Minerals suspended loading at a key Panama port for its Cobre Panama mine. news_summary: Copper rose on Wednesday as a weaker U.S. dollar followed perceived dovish comments from Fed Chair Powell and as supply disruptions supported prices. LME three-month copper gained 0.7% and Shanghai March rose 0.7%. Chile export declines and the resumption of Peru’s Antapaccay operations were cited. | Anglo American will buy a 9.9% stake in Canada Nickel, owner of the Crawford nickel project in Ontario. The miner will contribute technology and obtain exclusive rights to buy up to 10% of recoveries from nickel concentrate and related materials, supporting expected nickel output expansion.

news_summary: SMM expects nickel prices to stay rangebound with some downward potential. Increased profitability and expected flows of imported pure nickel plates should ease supply tightness in the coming weeks. Nickel ore costs are firm, but stainless steel cold-rolled demand is weaker, with some restocking by alloy makers after recent price declines. | SMM reports that on Feb 9 spot pure nickel premiums fell for both Jinchuan and NORNICKEL (each down about 600 yuan/mt day-on-day). Nickel inquiries and transactions were sluggish, with lower premiums as NORNICKEL supply arrived steadily. Nickel briquette trading was weak versus nickel sulphate prices. | USGS preliminary estimates show global nickel ore output rose 21% in 2022 to 3.3 million mt, driven by a 54% jump in Indonesia to 1.6 million mt. The increase is attributed to commissioning of integrated NPI and stainless steel projects, plus ongoing development of nickel-cobalt intermediates for battery-grade nickel sulphate feedstock. | China produced 31,200 mt Ni content of nickel sulphate in January, down 16.99% month-on-month, as precursor plant output cuts and COVID disruption reduced operating rates and shipments. High inventories dampened demand. February output is expected to rebound, but near-term supply remains pressured. | SMM reports that LME nickel inventories fell by 936 mt to 48,252 mt on Feb 9. The decline signals tighter exchange availability for nickel, which can support near-term price expectations as market participants adjust for reduced stock levels. | EV Nickel began Phase 4 drilling at its Shaw Dome W4 Zone, targeting the eastern expansion of previously defined high-grade nickel mineralization and collecting metallurgical samples. Assay results are expected in early Q2. The company says results could materially impact the upcoming updated resource estimate. | Anglo American agreed to buy a 9.9% stake in Canada Nickel for C$24m and will provide technical support for the Crawford nickel project in Ontario. The equity funding is meant to complete permitting, and Anglo gains rights related to recoveries of nickel concentrate and related carbon credits. | Trafigura says it faces a $577 million loss after discovering nickel shipment fraud, with some purchased cargoes missing the nickel they were supposed to contain. The fraud was detected through inspections and legal action has started, while most shipments remain in transit awaiting further checks. | Trafigura says it may lose up to $577mn after suing parties including TMT Metals over an alleged “systematic fraud” in containerised nickel shipments during 2022. Some containers inspected since late Dec 2022 were found not to contain nickel. Most shipments remain in transit pending inspection.

news_summary: Bloomberg reports GM is in advanced negotiations with Vale for a stake in Vale’s newly established base metals division, which supplies nickel and copper for EV batteries. With automakers scaling EV production, Vale expects sizable growth in nickel and copper demand, and GM’s bids could tighten expectations despite no deal being finalized. | Aperam and Econick formed a JV, Botanickel, to produce biosourced nickel by extracting nickel from hyperaccumulator plants and feeding it into stainless-steel recycling and processing routes. The company claims pilot production has started and aims to cover part of primary nickel demand, potentially reducing exposure to nickel volatility. | Copper eased on China factory-gate price weakness and weaker U.S. employment data that raised demand and recession worries, while Las Bambas in Peru maintained reduced output after road blockades. Nickel surged after Trafigura disclosed a large nickel-fraud-related loss and rising expectations around low-stock liquidity. Aluminium also edged lower. | Copper fell as China factory-gate prices and weaker U.S. unemployment claims revived demand and recession worries. Nickel jumped after Trafigura disclosed a large charge tied to alleged systematic fraud involving nickel cargoes. Aluminium also eased on the day, indicating a mixed but mainly risk-driven tape across industrial metals. | Trafigura will record a ~$577m impairment after discovering missing nickel in containerized shipments from suppliers allegedly involved in document misrepresentation. The issue is described as isolated and under review, with inspections ongoing for remaining in-transit loads. Trafigura initiated legal proceedings against those parties. | The piece reviews the LME’s efforts to restart and stabilize nickel trading after the March 2022 volatility suspension, including possible permission to resume Asian hours, reworking margin/15% daily limits, improved OTC oversight, and clearing access proposals. Ongoing sanctions-related nerves around Russian deliverable nickel also shape sentiment. | SMM reports Chinese nickel ore inventories fell 267,000 wmt WoW to 7.83 million wmt, with Ni content down 2,000 mt to about 62,000 mt. Shipments from the Philippines were constrained by rainy-season conditions, and rising February NPI output is expected to lift ore consumption. | The piece discusses Indonesia’s dominant nickel position—21 million tonnes of proven reserves, an ongoing nickel ore export ban, and rapidly expanding processed nickel and battery/EV deals. It also notes efforts to persuade Tesla to produce EVs or batteries in Indonesia, reinforcing expectations for nickel-linked demand. | Trafigura announced a $577 million impairment after discovering that some purchased containers did not contain nickel as contracted, firing its head of nickel/cobalt trading and pursuing legal action. With a portion of shipments already inspected and failing checks, while others remain in transit, uncertainty around nickel supply quality increases.

news_summary: London copper edged up 0.2% but stayed near a one-week low as traders were cautious ahead of U.S. CPI. The dollar’s rise weighed, but China bank-loan data supported demand hopes. LME aluminium also rose 0.4%, while SHFE nickel fell 3.4% amid weaker sentiment. | Fastmarkets reports nickel sulfate prices in China rebounded 3.45% week-on-week after Tsingshan began producing nickel full plate using nickel sulfate, boosting sentiment and pressuring futures. But traders remain skeptical that EV-driven demand will strengthen, citing slowing battery metal demand and expectations of ample spot supply. | Trafigura alleges large-scale containerized nickel fraud by traders connected to Prateek Gupta, involving 20,000 tonnes of uncut nickel cathode and potential $577m losses. After the release, LME three-month nickel saw sharp volatility and a temporary spike, then eased, while participants fear wider pricing and trade-finance impacts.

news_summary: Bloomberg reports that Citi stopped financing Trafigura’s nickel “transit finance” trades on Oct. 27 after concerns over alleged fraud tied to a counterparty. Trafigura’s inspections found containers labelled as nickel did not contain nickel, and it previously sold some of these cargoes onward, potentially amplifying nickel market disruption fears. | China’s nickel ore imports fell to 2.76 million mt in Dec 2022, down 33.9% MoM, while port stockpiles continued declining. Philippine ore shipments dropped 22.7% MoM due to rainy-season disruptions. Sulphide ore imports also declined. January import levels are expected to stay low despite lower ocean freight and a NPI price rebound. | SMM reports China’s refined nickel imports totaled 14,441 mt in Dec 2022, down 0.55% MoM and 46.41% YoY. Most volume came from other unwrought alloy nickel, which fell sharply year-on-year. Russia was the largest supplier. Separate categories showed mixed MoM/YoY movements.
